This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 12, 2026.

Between:
Quantum Dynamics Ltd., registered at 78 Innovation Way, Dublin 2, Ireland
Company Reg: IE-567890
("Disclosing Party")

And:
Arcturus Ventures GmbH, Friedrichstraße 112, 10117 Berlin, Germany
HRB: 234567 B
("Receiving Party")

1. PURPOSE
The parties wish to explore a potential strategic partnership involving Quantum Dynamics' proprietary quantum computing algorithms and Arcturus Ventures' commercial distribution network in the DACH region.

2. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All technical specifications, algorithms, source code, business plans, financial projections, customer lists, pricing models, and any information marked "Confidential" or reasonably understood to be confidential.

3. OBLIGATIONS
The Receiving Party shall:
a) Use Confidential Information solely for evaluating the potential partnership
b) Not disclose to third parties without prior written consent
c) Limit access to employees with need-to-know, who are bound by similar obligations
d) Return or destroy all materials upon written request within 14 days

4. EXCLUSIONS
Information that: (a) is publicly available, (b) was known prior to disclosure, (c) is independently developed, (d) is required by law or court order (with prompt notice to Disclosing Party).

5. TERM
This Agreement is effective for 3 years from the date of signing. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after termination.

6. REMEDIES
The Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other legal remedies.

7. GOVERNING LAW
Laws of Ireland. Jurisdiction: Courts of Dublin.

Signed:
Dr. Fiona McCarthy, CEO — Quantum Dynamics Ltd.
Markus Weber, Managing Director — Arcturus Ventures GmbH

N° : CPS-2026-0198
Fait à Paris, le 10 mars 2026

Entre :
DigiWave SAS, 45 Avenue des Champs-Élysées, 75008 Paris
SIRET : 823 456 789 00015
Représentée par : Julien Moreau, Directeur Général
(ci-après « le Prestataire »)

Et :
Maison Laurent SARL, 12 Rue du Faubourg Saint-Honoré, 75008 Paris
SIRET : 912 345 678 00022
Représentée par : Claire Laurent, Gérante
(ci-après « le Client »)

Article 1 — Objet
Le Prestataire s'engage à concevoir et développer un site e-commerce sur mesure pour le Client, incluant : design UX/UI, développement front-end et back-end, intégration du système de paiement Stripe, module de gestion des stocks, et optimisation SEO.

Article 2 — Durée
Le projet sera réalisé en 4 phases sur une durée de 16 semaines, du 01.04.2026 au 22.07.2026.
Phase 1 (semaines 1-3) : Maquettes et validation design
Phase 2 (semaines 4-9) : Développement
Phase 3 (semaines 10-13) : Tests et corrections
Phase 4 (semaines 14-16) : Mise en production et formation

Article 3 — Prix
Montant total HT : 42 000,00 EUR
TVA (20%) : 8 400,00 EUR
Montant total TTC : 50 400,00 EUR

Échéancier de paiement :
- 30% à la signature : 12 600,00 EUR HT
- 30% à la validation du design : 12 600,00 EUR HT
- 40% à la livraison finale : 16 800,00 EUR HT

Article 4 — Propriété intellectuelle
Le Client devient propriétaire de l'ensemble des livrables après paiement intégral.

Article 5 — Pénalités de retard
0,5% du montant total HT par semaine de retard, plafonné à 10%.

Article 6 — Garantie
Garantie de 6 mois après livraison couvrant les bugs et dysfonctionnements.

Article 7 — Résiliation
Chaque partie peut résilier avec un préavis de 30 jours. En cas de résiliation par le Client, les phases achevées restent dues.

Article 8 — Droit applicable
Droit français. Tribunal compétent : Tribunal de Commerce de Paris.

Signatures :
Julien Moreau — DigiWave SAS
Claire Laurent — Maison Laurent SARL

HRB 234567

("Receiving Party")

1. PURPOSE
The parties wish to explore a potential joint research collaboration in the field of quantum computing error correction algorithms (the "Purpose").

2. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All technical data, research findings, algorithms, source code, prototypes, business strategies, financial information, and customer data disclosed by either party in connection with the Purpose.

Exclusions: information that (a) is publicly available, (b) was known prior to disclosure, (c) is independently developed, or (d) is required by law to be disclosed.

3. OBLIGATIONS
- Receiving Party shall use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose
- Receiving Party shall restrict access to employees and advisors with need-to-know, bound by similar obligations
- Receiving Party shall not reverse engineer any prototypes or samples
- Receiving Party shall return or destroy all materials within 30 days of written request

4. TERM
This Agreement is effective for 3 years from the date of execution. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after expiration.

5. REMEDIES
The Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other remedies available at law or equity.

6. GOVERNING LAW
Laws of England and Wales. Disputes subject to exclusive jurisdiction of courts in London.

7. NO LICENSE
No license or right under any patent, copyright, or trade secret is granted by this Agreement.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF:
Dr. Eleanor Price — Quantum Dynamics Ltd.
Prof. Dr. Klaus Weber — NovaTech Research GmbH

input:
CONTRAT DE PRESTATION DE SERVICES

N° du contrat : CPS-2026-0178
Fait à Paris, le 12 mars 2026

Entre :
DigiCraft Solutions SAS
25 Rue de Rivoli, 75001 Paris
SIRET : 123 456 789 00012
Représentée par : Mme Claire Dubois, Directrice Générale
Ci-après dénommée « le Prestataire »

Et :
Boulangerie Artisanale Martin SARL
48 Boulevard Saint-Germain, 75005 Paris
SIRET : 987 654 321 00034
Représentée par : M. Pierre Martin, Gérant
Ci-après dénommé « le Client »

Article 1 — Objet
Le Prestataire s'engage à concevoir, développer et déployer un site web e-commerce pour le Client, incluant : catalogue produits (jusqu'à 500 références), système de commande en ligne, paiement sécurisé (Stripe), gestion des livraisons, et interface d'administration.

Article 2 — Durée
La prestation s'étend du 1er avril au 30 juin 2026 (3 mois).
Phase 1 — Maquettes et validation : 1er–30 avril
Phase 2 — Développement : 1er mai–15 juin
Phase 3 — Tests et déploiement : 16–30 juin

Article 3 — Rémunération
Montant total : 24 000 € HT
- Acompte à la signature : 8 000 € HT (33%)
- Validation maquettes : 8 000 € HT (33%)
- Livraison finale : 8 000 € HT (34%)
TVA applicable : 20%
Montant TTC : 28 800 €
Paiement à 30 jours date de facture.

Article 4 — Pénalités de retard
En cas de retard de livraison imputable au Prestataire : 0,5% du montant total HT par semaine de retard, plafonné à 10%.

Article 5 — Propriété intellectuelle
Le code source et les éléments graphiques deviennent la propriété du Client après paiement intégral.

Article 6 — Maintenance
Maintenance corrective gratuite pendant 3 mois après livraison. Au-delà, contrat de maintenance optionnel à 400 € HT/mois.

Article 7 — Résiliation
Chaque partie peut résilier le contrat avec un préavis de 30 jours. En cas de résiliation par le Client, les sommes versées restent acquises au Prestataire.

Article 8 — Loi applicable
Droit français. Tribunal compétent : Tribunal de Commerce de Paris.

Signatures :
Claire Dubois — DigiCraft Solutions SAS
Pierre Martin — Boulangerie Artisanale Martin SARL

REGULAMIN USŁUGI PRZECHOWYWANIA DANYCH W CHMURZE "CLOUDSTORE"

obowiązujący od 01.03.2026 r.

Usługodawca: CloudStore Sp. z o.o., ul. Techniczna 5, 02-676 Warszawa, NIP 5213456789, KRS 0000456789

§1. DEFINICJE
Usługa — przechowywanie i udostępnianie danych w infrastrukturze chmurowej Usługodawcy. Użytkownik — osoba fizyczna lub prawna korzystająca z Usługi. Dane — pliki, dokumenty i inne treści przesłane przez Użytkownika.

§2. ZAKRES USŁUGI
Usługodawca zapewnia: przechowywanie danych z redundancją (min. 3 kopie w 2 centrach danych), szyfrowanie AES-256 w spoczynku i TLS 1.3 w transmisji, dostępność 99,95% w skali miesiąca (SLA), automatyczne kopie zapasowe co 24h.

§3. PLANY I CENNIK
- Basic: 100 GB, 2 użytkowników — 29 PLN/mies.
- Professional: 1 TB, 10 użytkowników — 99 PLN/mies.
- Enterprise: 10 TB, bez limitu użytkowników — 499 PLN/mies.
VAT doliczany zgodnie z obowiązującą stawką.

§4. DANE OSOBOWE
Przetwarzanie zgodne z RODO. Dane przechowywane wyłącznie na terenie EOG (centra danych: Warszawa, Frankfurt). Usługodawca jest procesorem danych; administratorem jest Użytkownik.

§5. ODPOWIEDZIALNOŚĆ
Usługodawca odpowiada za utratę danych wyłącznie w przypadku udowodnionej winy umyślnej lub rażącego niedbalstwa. Maksymalna odpowiedzialność: 12-krotność miesięcznej opłaty. Brak odpowiedzialności za: szkody pośrednie, utracone korzyści, przerwy wynikające z siły wyższej.

§6. OGRANICZENIA
Zabronione: przechowywanie treści niezgodnych z prawem, materiałów naruszających prawa autorskie, złośliwego oprogramowania. Max rozmiar pojedynczego pliku: 50 GB.

§7. ROZWIĄZANIE
Użytkownik może rozwiązać umowę w dowolnym momencie (dane dostępne do pobrania przez 30 dni). Usługodawca może wypowiedzieć umowę z 30-dniowym wyprzedzeniem. Natychmiastowe rozwiązanie przy naruszeniu §6.

Kontakt: support@cloudstore.pl, tel. +48 22 123 45 67

LANDLORD:

EIN: 13-7654321

BrightWave Analytics Inc.
(a Delaware corporation)
Represented by: Sarah Mitchell, CEO

1. PREMISES
The Landlord hereby leases to Tenant approximately 4,800 sq ft of office space on the 12th floor (Suite 1205) at 580 Fifth Avenue, New York, NY 10036.

2. TERM
Initial term: 5 years, commencing July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2031.
Option to renew for two (2) additional 3-year terms upon 180 days' written notice.

3. RENT
- Year 1: $62.00/sq ft/year ($297,600/year; $24,800/month)
- Annual escalation: 3% per year
- Year 5 projected: $69.78/sq ft/year

4. SECURITY DEPOSIT
$74,400 (equivalent to 3 months' rent), held in escrow.

5. TENANT IMPROVEMENTS
Landlord provides a Tenant Improvement Allowance of $45/sq ft ($216,000 total). Improvements exceeding the allowance are Tenant's responsibility.

6. OPERATING EXPENSES
Tenant pays proportionate share (8.2%) of building operating expenses exceeding the 2026 base year.

7. INSURANCE
Tenant shall maintain: commercial general liability ($2,000,000 per occurrence), property insurance covering tenant improvements and personal property, workers' compensation as required by law.

8. TERMINATION
Early termination permitted after Year 3 with 12 months' notice and payment of unamortized TI costs plus 6 months' rent as termination fee.

9. GOVERNING LAW
State of New York.

Signatures:
James Chen, Managing Partner — Metro Properties LLC
Sarah Mitchell, CEO — BrightWave Analytics Inc.

Represented by: Dr. David Park, CTO

Receiving Party:
NexGen Robotics Ltd.
45 Innovation Way, Cambridge CB2 1TN, United Kingdom
Represented by: Emma Richardson, VP Engineering

1. PURPOSE
The parties wish to explore a potential joint development agreement for autonomous navigation systems (the "Project"). In connection with this evaluation, Disclosing Party may share proprietary technical information, algorithms, hardware specifications, and business strategies.

2. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
Includes but is not limited to: source code, technical documentation, patent applications (pending), prototype designs, customer lists, financial projections, and any information marked "Confidential" or "Proprietary."

Exclusions: (a) publicly available information, (b) information already known to Receiving Party, (c) independently developed information, (d) information received from a third party without restriction.

3. OBLIGATIONS
Receiving Party shall: (i) use Confidential Information solely for evaluating the Project, (ii) restrict access to employees/contractors with a need-to-know, (iii) apply at least the same degree of protection as its own confidential information (no less than reasonable care), (iv) not reverse-engineer any prototypes or samples provided.

4. TERM
This Agreement remains in effect for 3 years from the Effective Date. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after disclosure of each item of Confidential Information.

5. RETURN OF MATERIALS
Upon termination or request, Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information within 30 days and provide written certification of destruction.

6. REMEDIES
Breach may cause irreparable harm. Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity.

7. GOVERNING LAW
State of Texas, USA. Disputes resolved by binding arbitration in Austin, TX under AAA Commercial Rules.

8. NO LICENSE
No license or intellectual property rights are granted under this Agreement.

Signatures:
Dr. David Park, CTO — Quantum Dynamics Corp.
Emma Richardson, VP Engineering — NexGen Robotics Ltd.

1. ELIGIBILITY
Users must be at least 18 years old, resident in the UK or EEA, and pass identity verification (KYC) in accordance with the Money Laundering Regulations 2017.

2. ACCOUNT
Each user may hold one personal account. Business accounts available upon application. Account balance limit: £25,000 for standard accounts, £100,000 for verified business accounts.

3. TRANSACTIONS
- Domestic transfers: free, processed within 2 hours (Faster Payments)
- International transfers: 0.5% fee (min £1.50), processed within 1-3 business days
- Card payments: free for GBP transactions, 1.5% for foreign currency + Mastercard exchange rate
- ATM withdrawals: 2 free/month (UK), then £1.50/withdrawal. International: £2.00 + 1.5%
- Daily transaction limit: £10,000 (standard), £50,000 (business)

4. SECURITY
Two-factor authentication mandatory. Biometric login supported. FinPay monitors for suspicious activity under PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication requirements. Users must report unauthorized transactions within 13 months.

5. FUNDS PROTECTION
Client funds are safeguarded in segregated accounts at Barclays Bank in accordance with the Payment Services Regulations 2017. Funds are not covered by FSCS deposit protection.

6. FEES
Monthly maintenance: free. Inactivity fee: £3/month after 12 months of no transactions. Account closure: free.

7. TERMINATION
FinPay may suspend or close accounts for: suspected fraud, regulatory non-compliance, violation of these Terms, or court/regulatory order. Users may close accounts at any time; remaining balance refunded within 5 business days.

8. LIABILITY
FinPay is not liable for: losses from unauthorized access where user failed to protect credentials, service interruptions due to force majeure, delays in third-party payment networks.

9. COMPLAINTS
Email: complaints@finpay.co.uk. Response within 15 business days. If unresolved, escalate to the Financial Ombudsman Service.

10. GOVERNING LAW
Laws of England and Wales.

ARTICLE 1 — OBJET
Le Prestataire s'engage à réaliser un audit complet de cybersécurité de l'infrastructure informatique du Client, incluant : tests de pénétration (réseau interne et externe), audit de conformité RGPD, évaluation des vulnérabilités applicatives, revue de la politique de sécurité, et formation du personnel (2 sessions de 4 heures).

ARTICLE 2 — DURÉE
Prestation du 01.04.2026 au 30.06.2026 (3 mois). Rapport final livré au plus tard le 15.07.2026.

ARTICLE 3 — PRIX

- Phase 1 (audit technique) : 35 000 € HT
- Phase 2 (conformité RGPD) : 18 500 € HT
- Phase 3 (formation + rapport) : 14 000 € HT
TVA applicable : 20%
Montant TTC : 81 000 €

Paiement : 30% à la signature (20 250 € HT), 40% à la livraison Phase 1, 30% à la livraison du rapport final.

ARTICLE 4 — CONFIDENTIALITÉ
Le Prestataire s'engage à maintenir la stricte confidentialité de toutes les informations découvertes durant l'audit. Durée de l'obligation : 5 ans après la fin de la prestation. Pénalité en cas de violation : 100 000 €.

ARTICLE 5 — RESPONSABILITÉ
Responsabilité du Prestataire limitée au montant du contrat. Assurance responsabilité civile professionnelle : 2 000 000 €.

ARTICLE 6 — RÉSILIATION
Résiliation possible par chaque partie avec préavis de 30 jours. En cas de résiliation anticipée par le Client, les phases achevées restent dues.

Loi applicable : Droit français. Tribunal compétent : Tribunal de commerce de Lyon.

Signatures :
Marc Dupont — CyberSec France SAS
Claire Martin — Groupe Alimentaire Provence SA

Paseo de la Castellana 180, 28046 Madrid
CIF: A-12345678
Representada por: Dra. Isabel Moreno Ruiz, Directora de Operaciones
En adelante, "el Cliente"

CLÁUSULA 1 — OBJETO
El Desarrollador se compromete a diseñar, desarrollar y entregar una aplicación web y móvil (iOS/Android) para gestión de citas médicas, historiales clínicos electrónicos y telemedicina, denominada "SaludConnect".

CLÁUSULA 2 — ALCANCE
Fase 1 (meses 1-3): Diseño UX/UI + backend API
Fase 2 (meses 4-6): Desarrollo app móvil + integración HL7/FHIR
Fase 3 (meses 7-8): Testing, corrección de errores, despliegue
Fase 4 (meses 9-12): Soporte post-lanzamiento y mantenimiento

CLÁUSULA 3 — PRECIO
Precio total: 285.000 € + IVA (21%)
- Fase 1: 85.000 €
- Fase 2: 120.000 €
- Fase 3: 45.000 €
- Fase 4: 35.000 €
Pago: 20% al inicio de cada fase, 80% a la entrega y aceptación.

CLÁUSULA 4 — PROPIEDAD INTELECTUAL
Todos los derechos de propiedad intelectual del software desarrollado pertenecerán al Cliente tras el pago completo. El Desarrollador podrá reutilizar componentes genéricos (frameworks, librerías propias) en otros proyectos.

CLÁUSULA 5 — GARANTÍA
12 meses de garantía sobre defectos de programación. Tiempo de respuesta para errores críticos: 4 horas. Corrección: 24 horas.

CLÁUSULA 6 — PENALIZACIONES
Retraso en la entrega: 0,5% del valor de la fase por cada semana de retraso, hasta un máximo del 10%.

CLÁUSULA 7 — PROTECCIÓN DE DATOS
Cumplimiento del RGPD. El Desarrollador actuará como encargado del tratamiento. Datos de salud tratados conforme al art. 9 RGPD.

CLÁUSULA 8 — RESOLUCIÓN
Cualquiera de las partes podrá resolver el contrato con 60 días de preaviso. Resolución inmediata por incumplimiento grave tras requerimiento de 15 días.

Ley aplicable: Legislación española. Jurisdicción: Juzgados de Madrid.

Firmas:
Alejandro García Torres — CodeFactory España S.L.
Dra. Isabel Moreno Ruiz — Clínica Salud Integral S.A.

Dati identificativi (nome, cognome, codice fiscale), dati di contatto (email, telefono, indirizzo), dati di navigazione (IP, cookie, preferenze), dati di acquisto (storico ordini, metodi di pagamento), dati biometrici (solo per il servizio Virtual Try-On, previo consenso esplicito).

a) Esecuzione del contratto di vendita e gestione ordini

- Cookie di profilazione: max 12 mesi

Fornitori di servizi IT (hosting: AWS, regione eu-south-1 Milano), corrieri (DHL, BRT), payment processor (Stripe), piattaforma email marketing (Mailchimp — trasferimento USA con Clausole Contrattuali Standard). Nessuna vendita di dati a terzi.

Accesso, rettifica, cancellazione, limitazione, portabilità, opposizione, revoca del consenso. Reclamo al Garante per la Protezione dei Dati Personali.

Mailchimp (USA): Clausole Contrattuali Standard ex art. 46 GDPR. Tutti gli altri dati trattati esclusivamente nell'UE.

7. DECISIONI AUTOMATIZZATE
La profilazione per raccomandazioni prodotto non produce effetti giuridici significativi. L'utente può richiedere l'intervento umano.

Data: 5 de março de 2026
Local: Lisboa

ENTRE:
Iberian Consulting Lda.
Avenida da Liberdade 245, 3º andar, 1250-143 Lisboa
NIPC: 514567890

Adiante designada "Consultora"

E:
Porto Logistics S.A.
Rua do Comércio 78, 4000-150 Porto
NIPC: 506789012
Representada por: Eng.ª Ana Oliveira, Diretora de Operações
Adiante designada "Cliente"

CLÁUSULA 1.ª — OBJETO
A Consultora compromete-se a prestar serviços de consultoria em transformação digital e otimização de processos logísticos, incluindo: diagnóstico operacional, redesenho de processos (metodologia Lean Six Sigma), seleção e implementação de sistema WMS (Warehouse Management System), e formação de equipas (até 50 colaboradores).

CLÁUSULA 2.ª — PRAZO
Início: 1 de abril de 2026
Duração: 9 meses (até 31 de dezembro de 2026)
Fase 1 — Diagnóstico (2 meses): abril-maio
Fase 2 — Redesenho (3 meses): junho-agosto
Fase 3 — Implementação WMS (3 meses): setembro-novembro
Fase 4 — Formação e go-live (1 mês): dezembro

CLÁUSULA 3.ª — HONORÁRIOS
Valor total: €142.000 + IVA (23%)
- Fase 1: €28.000
- Fase 2: €38.000
- Fase 3: €52.000
- Fase 4: €24.000
Pagamento: 50% no início de cada fase, 50% na entrega dos deliverables.

CLÁUSULA 4.ª — OBRIGAÇÕES DA CONSULTORA
a) Equipa dedicada: 1 Partner, 2 Senior Consultants, 1 Analyst
b) Relatórios mensais de progresso
c) KPIs mensuráveis: redução de 20% nos custos operacionais, aumento de 30% na eficiência de picking

CLÁUSULA 5.ª — CONFIDENCIALIDADE
Ambas as partes comprometem-se a manter sigilo sobre informações comerciais e operacionais durante 3 anos após o término do contrato. Penalidade: €75.000 por violação.

CLÁUSULA 6.ª — RESPONSABILIDADE
Responsabilidade da Consultora limitada ao valor total do contrato. Seguro de responsabilidade civil profissional: €1.500.000.

CLÁUSULA 7.ª — RESCISÃO
Rescisão com aviso prévio de 60 dias. Em caso de rescisão pelo Cliente, as fases concluídas permanecem devidas. Rescisão imediata por incumprimento grave após notificação de 15 dias.

Lei aplicável: Lei portuguesa.
Foro: Tribunal da Comarca de Lisboa.

Assinaturas:
Dr. Miguel Ferreira — Iberian Consulting Lda.
Eng.ª Ana Oliveira — Porto Logistics S.A.

Company No: 09876543

And:
Nordic Retail Group AS
Storgata 42, 0182 Oslo, Norway
Org. Nr: 987 654 321

1. Services Covered
Managed cloud infrastructure including: compute instances (up to 200 vCPUs), object storage (50 TB), managed PostgreSQL database cluster (3 nodes), CDN (European PoPs), 24/7 monitoring and incident response.

2. Service Levels
a) Platform Availability: 99.95% monthly uptime (excl. scheduled maintenance)
b) Scheduled Maintenance: max 4 hours/month, Sundays 02:00–06:00 CET, notified 72h in advance
c) Incident Response Times:
   - P1 (service down): 15 minutes
   - P2 (degraded): 1 hour
   - P3 (minor issue): 8 business hours
   - P4 (request): 2 business days
d) Data Durability: 99.999999999% (11 nines) for object storage
e) Backup: Daily automated backups, 30-day retention, RPO 1 hour, RTO 4 hours

3. Service Credits
If monthly uptime falls below guaranteed level:
- 99.0%–99.95%: 10% credit on monthly fee
- 95.0%–99.0%: 25% credit
- Below 95.0%: 50% credit
Credits capped at 50% of monthly fee. Must be claimed within 30 days.

4. Financial Terms
Monthly fee: £12,500 + VAT
Payment terms: Net 30
Annual commitment with 90-day termination notice
Early termination fee: 3 months' fees

5. Exclusions
SLA does not apply during: force majeure, customer-caused outages, third-party DNS failures, or scheduled maintenance windows.

6. Governance
Quarterly service review meetings. Monthly uptime reports provided by the 5th business day.

Signed:
James Crawford — CloudNine Infrastructure Ltd.
Ingrid Halvorsen — Nordic Retail Group AS
Date: March 20, 2026

Effective Date: January 1, 2026
Last Updated: March 10, 2026
Version: 3.2

StreamVault Inc.
580 Howard Street, San Francisco, CA 94105

1. Acceptance
By creating an account or using StreamVault services, you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.

2. Service Description
StreamVault provides cloud-based video hosting, transcoding, and streaming services for businesses. Plans: Starter ($49/mo, 500 GB storage, 2 TB bandwidth), Professional ($199/mo, 5 TB storage, 20 TB bandwidth), Enterprise (custom pricing).

3. Account Responsibilities
- You must be 18+ or authorized representative of an organization
- You are responsible for all activity under your account
- You must maintain accurate billing information
- Multi-factor authentication required for Enterprise accounts

4. Content Policy
- You retain ownership of all uploaded content
- You grant StreamVault a limited license to process, store, transcode, and deliver your content
- Prohibited: illegal content, malware, content violating third-party IP rights
- StreamVault may remove content violating this policy without notice

5. Payment & Billing
- Billed monthly in advance via credit card or ACH
- Overages billed at: storage $0.05/GB/month, bandwidth $0.08/GB
- 15-day grace period for failed payments before suspension
- No refunds for partial months; annual plans refundable pro-rata minus 20% early termination fee

6. Data & Privacy
- Data stored in US-West and EU-West regions
- GDPR and CCPA compliant
- Data Processing Agreement available upon request
- Data retained 30 days after account termination, then permanently deleted

7. Limitation of Liability
StreamVault's total liability shall not exceed fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim. StreamVault is not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

8. Termination
- Either party may terminate with 30 days' written notice
- StreamVault may suspend immediately for ToS violations or non-payment
- Upon termination, you have 30 days to export your data

9. Governing Law
State of California, USA. Disputes resolved through binding arbitration (AAA rules).

Contact: legal@streamvault.io

LETTER OF INTENT

Date: March 15, 2026

From:
Atlas Ventures Partners
1200 Fifth Avenue, Suite 3200
New York, NY 10029

To:
QuantumLeap Robotics Inc.
3400 Hillview Avenue
Palo Alto, CA 94304
Attn: Dr. Elena Vasquez, CEO

Re: Proposed Series B Investment in QuantumLeap Robotics Inc.

Dear Dr. Vasquez,

Atlas Ventures Partners ("Atlas") is pleased to present this non-binding Letter of Intent outlining the principal terms of a proposed Series B equity investment in QuantumLeap Robotics Inc. ("Company").

1. Investment Amount: $25,000,000
2. Pre-Money Valuation: $100,000,000
3. Post-Money Valuation: $125,000,000
4. Securities: Series B Preferred Stock
5. Price Per Share: $8.33 (based on 15,006,002 shares outstanding)
6. Lead Investor: Atlas Ventures Partners ($18,000,000)
7. Co-Investors: Horizon Growth Fund ($5,000,000), Stellar Angels ($2,000,000)

8. Key Terms:
   a) Board Seat: Atlas to receive one board seat
   b) Liquidation Preference: 1x non-participating preferred
   c) Anti-Dilution: Broad-based weighted average
   d) Pro-Rata Rights: All Series B investors
   e) Protective Provisions: Standard (sale of company, new debt >$5M, changes to charter)
   f) Information Rights: Monthly financials, annual audited statements, annual budget
   g) ROFR & Co-Sale: Standard
   h) Drag-Along: Majority of preferred + majority of common
   i) Vesting: All unvested founder shares subject to 4-year vesting, 1-year cliff

9. Use of Proceeds:
   - R&D expansion (60%): hire 25 engineers, expand robotics lab
   - Go-to-market (25%): US enterprise sales team, 3 pilot programs
   - Working capital (15%)

10. Exclusivity: 45-day exclusivity period from execution of this LOI
11. Due Diligence: 30-day period commencing upon execution
12. Expected Closing: May 15, 2026

This LOI is non-binding except for Sections 10 (Exclusivity) and confidentiality provisions, which shall be binding and governed by the laws of the State of Delaware.

Sincerely,
Marcus Thompson
Managing Partner, Atlas Ventures Partners

Royal Dutch Engineering N.V.
Weena 505, 3013 AL Rotterdam
KvK: 87654321
Vertegenwoordigd door: Mw. Annemarie de Vries, CTO

Artikel 1 — Doel
Partijen wensen vertrouwelijke informatie uit te wisselen met betrekking tot de ontwikkeling van een waterstof-elektrolyse systeem voor industriële toepassingen (Project "HydroGen-X").

Artikel 2 — Vertrouwelijke Informatie
Alle technische specificaties, onderzoeksresultaten, bedrijfsgegevens, financiële data, klantenlijsten, en commerciële strategieën die schriftelijk, elektronisch of mondeling worden gedeeld en als vertrouwelijk zijn aangemerkt.

Artikel 3 — Verplichtingen
a) Vertrouwelijke informatie uitsluitend gebruiken voor het genoemde doel
b) Toegang beperken tot medewerkers die direct betrokken zijn bij het project (max. 8 personen per partij)
c) Opslag in versleutelde systemen (minimaal AES-256)
d) Geen reverse engineering van prototypes of monsters
e) Teruggave of vernietiging van alle materialen binnen 21 dagen na beëindiging

Artikel 4 — Duur
Deze overeenkomst is geldig voor 24 maanden vanaf ondertekening.
De geheimhoudingsplicht blijft van kracht gedurende 5 jaar na het einde van de overeenkomst.

Artikel 5 — Boetebeding
Bij overtreding: boete van € 250.000 per incident, onverminderd het recht op aanvullende schadevergoeding.

Artikel 6 — Uitzonderingen
Informatie die: (a) reeds openbaar was, (b) onafhankelijk ontwikkeld, (c) rechtmatig van derden verkregen, (d) op grond van wettelijke verplichting moet worden onthuld.

Artikel 7 — Toepasselijk Recht
Nederlands recht. Geschillen: Rechtbank Amsterdam.

Getekend:
Pieter van den Berg — GreenTech Innovations B.V.
Annemarie de Vries — Royal Dutch Engineering N.V.

SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT (SLA)

Agreement Number: SLA-2026-EU-0412
Effective Date: April 1, 2026

Between:
Nebula Cloud Services Ltd.
88 Canary Wharf, London E14 5AB, United Kingdom
Company No.: 09876543
(hereinafter "Provider")

And:
MedTech Solutions GmbH
Friedrichstraße 112, 10117 Berlin, Germany
HRB 234567 B
(hereinafter "Client")

1. SCOPE OF SERVICES
Provider shall deliver managed cloud infrastructure services including:
- Dedicated Kubernetes cluster (16 nodes, 256 vCPU, 1TB RAM)
- Managed PostgreSQL database (HA configuration, 5TB storage)
- Object storage (S3-compatible, 50TB)
- 24/7 monitoring and incident response
- Monthly security patching and quarterly penetration testing

2. SERVICE LEVELS
- Uptime guarantee: 99.95% monthly (excl. scheduled maintenance)
- Scheduled maintenance windows: Sundays 02:00-06:00 UTC (max 2 per month)
- Incident response times:
  * P1 (service down): 15 minutes
  * P2 (degraded): 30 minutes
  * P3 (minor issue): 4 hours
  * P4 (request): 1 business day
- RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 1 hour
- RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 5 minutes

3. FINANCIAL TERMS
- Monthly fee: €18,500
- Payment terms: Net 30
- Annual commitment with auto-renewal

4. SERVICE CREDITS
- 99.9%-99.95%: 5% monthly credit
- 99.0%-99.9%: 15% monthly credit
- Below 99.0%: 30% monthly credit
- Maximum credit: 30% of monthly fee
- Credits applied to next invoice upon written request within 30 days

5. DATA PROTECTION
- All data stored in EU (Frankfurt region)
- GDPR compliant processing
- ISO 27001 certified infrastructure
- Annual SOC 2 Type II audit reports provided
- Data encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3)

6. TERMINATION
- Either party: 90 days written notice before renewal date
- Provider breach of SLA for 3 consecutive months: Client may terminate immediately
- Data export: Provider shall assist with migration for up to 30 days post-termination at no additional cost

Signed:
James Harrison, CTO — Nebula Cloud Services Ltd.
Dr. Katrin Bauer, VP Engineering — MedTech Solutions GmbH

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 1, 2026

Between:
Quantum Analytics Inc.
200 West Adams Street, Suite 1500, Chicago, IL 60606
("Disclosing Party")

And:
BrightPath Consulting LLC
750 Third Avenue, 9th Floor, New York, NY 10017
("Receiving Party")

1. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All non-public information disclosed by Quantum Analytics, including but not limited to: proprietary algorithms, machine learning models, client datasets, financial projections, product roadmaps, source code, API specifications, and business strategies.

2. OBLIGATIONS
The Receiving Party agrees to:
a) Hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence
b) Not disclose to any third party without prior written consent
c) Use information solely for the purpose of evaluating a potential strategic partnership ("Permitted Purpose")
d) Limit access to employees and advisors with a need-to-know basis, who are bound by similar confidentiality obligations
e) Return or destroy all materials within 10 business days upon written request

3. EXCLUSIONS
This Agreement does not apply to information that:
a) Is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party
b) Was known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure
c) Is independently developed without reference to Confidential Information
d) Is required to be disclosed by law or court order (with prompt notice to Disclosing Party)

4. TERM
- Agreement duration: 2 years from effective date (until March 1, 2028)
- Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after disclosure of each piece of information

5. REMEDIES
The Receiving Party acknowledges that breach may cause irreparable harm. The Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity.

6. GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Illinois. Any disputes shall be resolved in the federal or state courts located in Cook County, Illinois.

7. NON-SOLICITATION
During the term and for 12 months thereafter, the Receiving Party shall not solicit or hire any employee of the Disclosing Party.

Signed:
Dr. Michael Chen, CEO — Quantum Analytics Inc.
Sarah Goldstein, Managing Partner — BrightPath Consulting LLC

SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT (SLA)

Agreement No: SLA-2026-00193
Effective Date: April 1, 2026

Between:
Nexus Cloud Services Inc.
2100 Embarcadero Road, Suite 400, Palo Alto, CA 94303
("Service Provider")

And:
Atlantic Financial Group plc
25 Canary Wharf, London E14 5AB, United Kingdom
("Client")

1. SCOPE OF SERVICES
Service Provider shall deliver managed cloud infrastructure services including:
- Dedicated Kubernetes cluster management (up to 200 nodes)
- Managed PostgreSQL and Redis databases
- CDN and edge caching (global, 45 PoPs)
- DDoS mitigation and WAF
- 24/7 Security Operations Center (SOC) monitoring

2. SERVICE LEVELS
| Metric                    | Target      | Measurement     |
|---------------------------|-------------|------------------|
| Uptime (Infrastructure)   | 99.95%      | Monthly          |
| Uptime (Database)         | 99.9%       | Monthly          |
| Latency (API p95)         | < 150ms     | Rolling 5-min    |
| Incident Response (P1)    | < 15 min    | Per incident     |
| Incident Response (P2)    | < 1 hour    | Per incident     |
| Incident Response (P3)    | < 4 hours   | Per incident     |
| RTO (Recovery Time)       | < 30 min    | Per incident     |
| RPO (Recovery Point)      | < 5 min     | Per incident     |

3. SERVICE CREDITS
Failure to meet SLA targets entitles Client to service credits:
- 99.9% - 99.95%: 10% of monthly fee
- 99.5% - 99.9%: 25% of monthly fee
- Below 99.5%: 50% of monthly fee
- Two consecutive months below 99.5%: Client may terminate without penalty

4. FEES
Monthly service fee: $87,500 USD
Annual commitment: $1,050,000 USD (includes 2-month discount)
Payment terms: Net 30

5. TERM AND TERMINATION
Initial term: 24 months
Auto-renewal: 12-month periods
Termination notice: 120 days prior to renewal
Early termination fee: remaining months at 50% of monthly fee

6. COMPLIANCE
Service Provider maintains: SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, PCI DSS Level 1, GDPR compliance.

7. GOVERNING LAW
State of Delaware, United States.

Signatures:
David Chen, CEO — Nexus Cloud Services Inc.
Sarah Mitchell, CTO — Atlantic Financial Group plc

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 1, 2026.

Between:
Quantum Robotics Ltd.
78 Innovation Way, Cambridge CB2 1TN, United Kingdom
Company No: 12345678
("Disclosing Party")

And:
Precision Manufacturing GmbH
Industriestraße 42, 70569 Stuttgart, Germany
HRB 789012
("Receiving Party")

1. PURPOSE
The parties wish to explore a potential collaboration regarding the development and manufacturing of robotic actuator components (the "Purpose"). In connection with the Purpose, the Disclosing Party may share certain confidential and proprietary information.

2. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All technical, commercial, financial, and operational information disclosed by the Disclosing Party, including but not limited to: product designs, CAD files, manufacturing processes, algorithms, source code, customer lists, pricing structures, business plans, and patent applications.

3. OBLIGATIONS
The Receiving Party agrees to:
a) Hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence
b) Not disclose to any third party without prior written consent
c) Use the information solely for the Purpose
d) Limit internal access to employees with a need-to-know basis
e) Apply at least the same degree of care as for its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care

4. EXCLUSIONS
Information that: (a) is publicly available, (b) was known prior to disclosure, (c) is independently developed, or (d) is required by law or court order (with prompt notice to Disclosing Party).

5. TERM
This Agreement remains in effect for 3 years from the date of execution. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after termination.

6. RETURN OF MATERIALS
Upon termination or request, Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information within 30 days and provide written certification.

7. REMEDIES
Breach may cause irreparable harm. Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to other remedies. Liquidated damages: £250,000 per breach event.

8. GOVERNING LAW
Laws of England and Wales. Disputes: London Court of International Arbitration (LCIA).

Quantum Robotics Ltd. — Dr. Emily Watson, Director of R&D
Precision Manufacturing GmbH — Thomas Bauer, Geschäftsführer

O Prestador compromete-se a fornecer os seguintes serviços:

- Fase 1 (abr-mai): Levantamento de requisitos e arquitetura
- Fase 2 (jun-ago): Desenvolvimento e integração

- Fase 4 (out-nov): Formação, go-live e estabilização

O código-fonte desenvolvido especificamente para o Cliente é propriedade do Cliente.
Componentes reutilizáveis e frameworks permanecem propriedade do Prestador, com licença perpétua para o Cliente.

Cláusula 6.ª — PENALIDADES
Atraso na entrega: 0,5% do valor da fase por semana de atraso, até máximo de 10%.
Atraso no pagamento: juros de mora à taxa legal.

1. SERVICES COVERED
Managed cloud infrastructure including: dedicated Kubernetes clusters, managed PostgreSQL databases, object storage (S3-compatible), CDN, and 24/7 monitoring.

2. SERVICE LEVELS
- Platform Uptime: 99.95% monthly (excluding scheduled maintenance)
- Scheduled Maintenance Windows: Sundays 02:00–06:00 CT, with 72h advance notice
- Response Times:
  * P1 (Critical — service down): 15 minutes

  * P3 (Minor — non-critical): 4 hours
  * P4 (Informational): Next business day
- Resolution Times:
  * P1: 4 hours
  * P2: 8 hours
  * P3: 48 hours

3. SERVICE CREDITS
If monthly uptime falls below guaranteed level:
- 99.0%–99.95%: 10% credit on monthly fee
- 95.0%–99.0%: 25% credit
- Below 95.0%: 50% credit
Maximum aggregate credits: 30% of annual contract value.
Credits must be claimed within 30 days of the incident.

4. FINANCIAL TERMS
- Annual contract value: $186,000 ($15,500/month)
- Payment: Net 30, invoiced monthly
- Term: 24 months (April 1, 2026 — March 31, 2028)
- Early termination fee: 40% of remaining contract value

5. EXCLUSIONS
SLA does not apply to: force majeure, Client-caused outages, third-party DNS issues, attacks exceeding 10Gbps DDoS mitigation capacity.

6. GOVERNING LAW
State of Texas. Disputes resolved by binding arbitration (AAA rules).

Signed:
James Porter, VP Operations — NorthStar Cloud Services Inc.
Dr. Lisa Chen, CTO — Meridian Healthcare Group LLC

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 20, 2026.

Between:
Quantum Dynamics Ltd.
14 Canary Wharf Tower, London E14 5AB, United Kingdom
Company No: 12345678
("Disclosing Party")

And:
Atlas Robotics GmbH
Friedrichstraße 191, 10117 Berlin, Germany
HRB 234567 B
("Receiving Party")

1. PURPOSE
The parties wish to explore a potential collaboration in autonomous navigation systems for industrial robotics (the "Purpose"). In connection therewith, the Disclosing Party may share proprietary information with the Receiving Party.

2. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All technical, commercial, financial, and strategic information disclosed in writing, orally, or electronically, including but not limited to: algorithms, source code, hardware schematics, business plans, customer lists, pricing models, and patent applications.

3. OBLIGATIONS
The Receiving Party shall:
- Not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without prior written consent
- Use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose
- Limit access to employees with a need-to-know basis
- Apply at least the same degree of care as for its own confidential information (not less than reasonable care)
- Promptly notify the Disclosing Party of any unauthorized disclosure

4. EXCLUSIONS
Information that: (a) is publicly available, (b) was known prior to disclosure, (c) is independently developed, (d) is disclosed by a third party without restriction, (e) is required by law or court order (with prompt notice).

5. TERM
This Agreement is effective for 3 years from the date above. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after termination.

6. RETURN OF MATERIALS
Upon termination or request, the Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information within 30 days and certify destruction in writing.

7. REMEDIES
Breach may cause irreparable harm. The Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief without proving actual damages.

8. GOVERNING LAW
Laws of England and Wales. Exclusive jurisdiction: courts of London.

Signed:
Dr. Eleanor Voss, CTO — Quantum Dynamics Ltd.
Markus Weber, CEO — Atlas Robotics GmbH

input:
PRIVACY POLICY — DataPulse Analytics Platform

Effective: April 1, 2026
Last Updated: March 15, 2026

DataPulse Inc., 300 Montgomery Street, San Francisco, CA 94104 ("we", "us", "our") operates the DataPulse Analytics Platform (the "Service"). This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your information.

1. INFORMATION WE COLLECT
- Account Data: name, email, company, job title, phone number
- Usage Data: features used, session duration, click patterns, device/browser info, IP address
- Customer Data: data you upload for analysis (processed per our DPA)
- Payment Data: processed by Stripe; we store only last 4 digits and expiry
- Cookies: essential (session), analytics (Mixpanel), preference cookies

2. HOW WE USE YOUR DATA
- Provide and improve the Service
- Send transactional emails and product updates (opt-out available)
- Aggregate analytics for product development (anonymized)
- Comply with legal obligations
- Fraud prevention and security monitoring

3. DATA SHARING
We do NOT sell personal data. We share with:
- Sub-processors: AWS (hosting, us-east-1), Stripe (payments), Mixpanel (analytics), SendGrid (email)
- Law enforcement: only with valid legal process
- Business transfers: in case of merger/acquisition, with 30-day advance notice

4. DATA RETENTION
- Account data: duration of account + 90 days
- Usage data: 24 months, then anonymized
- Customer data: deleted within 30 days of account termination
- Backups: purged within 60 days of deletion

5. YOUR RIGHTS (GDPR / CCPA)
- Access, rectification, erasure, portability, restriction, objection
- CCPA: right to know, delete, opt-out of sale (we don't sell)
- Requests: privacy@datapulse.io, response within 30 days

6. SECURITY
SOC 2 Type II certified. AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit. Annual penetration testing by third party.

7. INTERNATIONAL TRANSFERS
EU–US Data Privacy Framework certified. SCCs for non-framework transfers.

8. CHILDREN
Service not directed to persons under 16. We do not knowingly collect data from minors.

9. CHANGES
Material changes notified 30 days in advance via email and in-app banner.

10. CONTACT
DPO: Sarah Mitchell, dpo@datapulse.io
Mailing: 300 Montgomery Street, San Francisco, CA 94104
output:

Cláusula 1.ª — Objeto
O Prestador compromete-se a desenvolver e implementar um sistema de gestão de armazém (WMS) customizado, incluindo: análise de requisitos, desenvolvimento de software, integração com ERP SAP existente, formação de utilizadores (20 colaboradores) e suporte pós-implementação.

Cláusula 2.ª — Prazo
Início: 01.04.2026. Conclusão: 30.09.2026 (6 meses).
Marcos:
- Fase 1 (análise): até 30.04.2026
- Fase 2 (desenvolvimento): até 31.07.2026
- Fase 3 (integração + testes): até 31.08.2026
- Fase 4 (formação + go-live): até 30.09.2026

Cláusula 3.ª — Preço e pagamento
- Valor total: 78.000 EUR (+ IVA 23%)
- 20% na assinatura (15.600 EUR)
- 30% na entrega da Fase 2 (23.400 EUR)
- 30% na conclusão da Fase 3 (23.400 EUR)
- 20% após aceitação final (15.600 EUR)
- Pagamento: 15 dias após emissão de fatura
- Conta: PT50 0035 0780 0000 1234 5678 9 (Caixa Geral de Depósitos)

Cláusula 4.ª — Garantia
O Prestador garante o funcionamento do software por 12 meses após a aceitação, incluindo correção de bugs sem custo adicional.

Cláusula 5.ª — Propriedade intelectual
O código-fonte e a documentação técnica são propriedade do Cliente após pagamento integral.

Cláusula 6.ª — Penalidades
Atraso na entrega: 0,5% do valor total por semana de atraso, até máximo de 10%.
Atraso no pagamento: juros de mora à taxa legal + 2 pontos percentuais.

Cláusula 7.ª — Confidencialidade
Ambas as partes comprometem-se à confidencialidade por 3 anos após o término do contrato.

Cláusula 8.ª — Resolução de litígios
Lei portuguesa. Foro: Tribunal da Comarca de Lisboa.

Effective Date: April 1, 2026

Between:

14 Canary Wharf, London E14 5AB, United Kingdom
Company No: 09876543

And:
Nordic Logistics AS
Strandveien 18, 0252 Oslo, Norway
Org.nr: 987 654 321

1. SERVICE DESCRIPTION
CloudBridge shall provide managed cloud infrastructure services including: dedicated Kubernetes clusters (3 production, 1 staging), managed PostgreSQL databases (2x primary with read replicas), CDN with 28 global edge locations, and 24/7 monitoring.

2. SERVICE LEVELS
- Uptime Guarantee: 99.95% monthly (excludes planned maintenance windows, max 4h/month)
- API Response Time: p95 < 200ms, p99 < 500ms
- Data Durability: 99.999999999% (11 nines)
- Incident Response: P1 (critical) — 15 min, P2 (major) — 1h, P3 (minor) — 4h, P4 (low) — next business day
- RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 1 hour
- RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 5 minutes

3. SERVICE CREDITS
If monthly uptime falls below guaranteed levels:
- 99.95% — 99.0%: 10% credit on monthly fee
- 99.0% — 95.0%: 25% credit
- Below 95.0%: 50% credit
Maximum credit: 50% of monthly fee. Credits must be claimed within 30 days.

4. PRICING
- Monthly fee: £18,500
- Payment terms: Net 30
- Annual commitment with 90-day termination notice
- Price review: annually, capped at CPI + 2%

5. EXCLUSIONS
SLA does not apply to: force majeure events, customer-caused outages, third-party DNS failures, or attacks exceeding 100 Gbps DDoS mitigation capacity.

6. GOVERNANCE
Quarterly service reviews. Dedicated account manager: James Crawford (j.crawford@cloudbridge.io).

Signed:
James Crawford, VP Sales — CloudBridge Infrastructure Ltd.
Erik Johansen, CTO — Nordic Logistics AS

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 20, 2026.

Between:
Quantum AI Research Inc.
2100 Sand Hill Road, Menlo Park, CA 94025
Represented by: Dr. Sarah Chen, Chief Research Officer

And:
DeepMind Technologies Limited
6 Pancras Square, London N1C 4AG, United Kingdom
Represented by: Dr. Robert Hayes, VP of Partnerships

1. PURPOSE
The parties wish to explore a potential joint research collaboration in quantum machine learning algorithms ("Purpose"). In connection with this Purpose, each party may disclose Confidential Information to the other.

2. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All non-public technical, business, or financial information disclosed by either party, including but not limited to: research data, algorithms, model architectures, training datasets, unpublished papers, patent applications, business plans, customer lists, and financial projections.

3. OBLIGATIONS
- Receiving party shall protect Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care as its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care
- Use solely for the Purpose described herein
- Limit disclosure to employees and contractors with a need to know, who are bound by similar obligations
- No reverse engineering of any disclosed technology

4. EXCLUSIONS
Information that: (a) is publicly available, (b) was known prior to disclosure, (c) is independently developed, (d) is disclosed by a third party without restriction.

5. TERM
This Agreement shall remain in effect for 3 years from the Effective Date. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after disclosure of each piece of Confidential Information.

6. RETURN/DESTRUCTION
Upon termination or written request, all Confidential Information and copies shall be returned or destroyed within 30 days, with written certification.

7. REMEDIES
Breach may cause irreparable harm. Disclosing party is entitled to seek injunctive relief without posting bond, in addition to other legal remedies.

8. GOVERNING LAW
State of California, USA. Disputes resolved by binding arbitration under JAMS rules in San Francisco.

Dr. Sarah Chen — Quantum AI Research Inc.
Dr. Robert Hayes — DeepMind Technologies Limited

QUARTERLY FINANCIAL REPORT

Company: Meridian HealthTech Inc.
NASDAQ: MHTI
Period: Q4 2025 (October 1 — December 31, 2025)
Report Date: February 15, 2026
Prepared by: CFO David Park

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Meridian HealthTech delivered strong Q4 results, exceeding analyst consensus on both revenue and earnings. The launch of our AI-powered diagnostic platform MedScan Pro drove significant growth in the enterprise segment.

FINANCIAL HIGHLIGHTS
- Revenue: $47.3M (+28% YoY, +12% QoQ)
  - SaaS recurring: $31.8M (67% of revenue)
  - Professional services: $10.2M
  - Hardware/devices: $5.3M
- Gross margin: 71.2% (vs 68.5% Q4 2024)
- Operating expenses: $29.1M
  - R&D: $14.2M (30% of revenue)
  - Sales & Marketing: $9.8M
  - G&A: $5.1M
- EBITDA: $12.8M (27.1% margin, vs 19.3% Q4 2024)
- Net income: $8.4M ($0.32 EPS diluted, vs $0.18 Q4 2024)
- Free cash flow: $10.1M

KEY METRICS
- Total customers: 2,340 (+15% YoY)
- Enterprise customers (>$100K ARR): 187 (+42% YoY)
- Net Revenue Retention: 128%
- Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR): $134.2M
- Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) payback: 14 months

NOTABLE DEVELOPMENTS
- MedScan Pro launched October 15, 2025 — 84 enterprise deals closed in Q4
- FDA 510(k) clearance received for cardiology module (November 2025)
- Acquired PathAI Analytics for $12.5M (AI pathology, 23 employees, integrated Q1 2026)
- Opened EMEA headquarters in Amsterdam (January 2026)

RISKS & CHALLENGES
- Increasing competition from established EHR vendors adding AI features
- Regulatory uncertainty around AI diagnostics in EU (MDR compliance)
- Key person risk: 3 of 5 principal scientists approaching retirement

GUIDANCE — FY2026
- Revenue: $210M — $220M (+18-24% YoY)
- EBITDA margin: 25-28%
- R&D investment: maintaining 28-32% of revenue

David Park, CFO
Meridian HealthTech Inc.

Article 1 — Objet
Le Prestataire s'engage à réaliser la migration de l'infrastructure e-commerce du Client vers une architecture microservices sur Kubernetes, incluant : audit de l'existant, conception de l'architecture cible, migration des données, développement des APIs, tests de performance et mise en production.

Article 2 — Durée
Durée du projet : 9 mois (1er avril 2026 — 31 décembre 2026)
Phase 1 — Audit et conception : avril-mai 2026
Phase 2 — Développement : juin-octobre 2026
Phase 3 — Tests et déploiement : novembre-décembre 2026

Article 3 — Prix et modalités de paiement
- Montant total : 840 000 € HT
- Paiement échelonné :
  - 20% à la signature (168 000 €)
  - 30% à la validation de la Phase 1 (252 000 €)
  - 30% à la validation de la Phase 2 (252 000 €)
  - 20% à la recette finale (168 000 €)
- TVA : 20%
- Délai de paiement : 30 jours fin de mois
- Pénalités de retard : 3× le taux d'intérêt légal

Article 4 — Équipe projet
- Chef de projet : 1 (temps plein)
- Architectes cloud : 2
- Développeurs seniors : 4
- DevOps : 2
- QA : 1
Taux journalier moyen : 950 € HT

Article 5 — Obligations du Client
- Fournir l'accès aux environnements existants sous 5 jours ouvrés
- Désigner un responsable projet côté Client
- Participer aux comités de pilotage bimensuels

Article 6 — Garantie
Garantie de 12 mois après la recette finale. Corrections des anomalies bloquantes sous 24h, majeures sous 72h.

Article 7 — Propriété intellectuelle
Les développements spécifiques réalisés dans le cadre du contrat deviennent la propriété du Client après paiement intégral.

Article 8 — Résiliation
Résiliation pour faute : mise en demeure par LRAR, délai de 30 jours pour remédier.
Résiliation pour convenance : préavis de 3 mois, indemnité égale à 20% du montant restant dû.

Tribunal compétent : Tribunal de Commerce de Paris

Antoine Dubois — TechSphere SAS
Isabelle Martin — Groupe Carrefour SA

4. Testy akceptacyjne i wdrożenie produkcyjne — do 31.08.2026
5. Wsparcie powdrożeniowe 12 miesięcy od odbioru

§3. Wynagrodzenie
Łączne wynagrodzenie ryczałtowe: 380 000 PLN netto + VAT 23%.
Płatne w 4 transzach: 95 000 PLN po zakończeniu każdego etapu (§2 pkt 1-4).
Termin płatności: 21 dni od wystawienia faktury.

§4. Kary umowne
a) Za opóźnienie Wykonawcy: 0,5% wartości opóźnionego etapu za każdy dzień, max 15%.
b) Za odstąpienie z winy Wykonawcy: 20% łącznego wynagrodzenia.
c) Za naruszenie poufności danych medycznych: 100 000 PLN.

§5. Poufność
Wykonawca zobowiązuje się do zachowania poufności wszelkich danych medycznych pacjentów. Przetwarzanie danych osobowych zgodnie z RODO — odrębna umowa powierzenia w Załączniku 2.

§6. Prawa autorskie
Wykonawca przenosi na Zamawiającego majątkowe prawa autorskie do wytworzonego oprogramowania z chwilą zapłaty ostatniej transzy.

§7. Wypowiedzenie
Każda ze stron może wypowiedzieć umowę z 60-dniowym okresem wypowiedzenia. Zamawiający płaci za zrealizowane etapy.

input:
SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT

Agreement No: SLA-2026-0312
Effective Date: April 1, 2026

Between:
CloudPeak Infrastructure Ltd.
200 Canary Wharf, London E14 5AB, United Kingdom
Company No: 09876543
(hereinafter "Provider")

And:
Nordic Logistics AS
Storgata 28, 0184 Oslo, Norway
Org.nr: 987 654 321
(hereinafter "Client")

1. SERVICES COVERED
This SLA covers the following managed services:
a) Dedicated cloud infrastructure (8 bare-metal servers, 512GB RAM each)
b) Managed Kubernetes cluster (production + staging)
c) Database hosting (PostgreSQL 16 cluster, 3-node HA)
d) CDN and DDoS protection
e) 24/7 Network Operations Center (NOC) monitoring

2. SERVICE LEVELS
| Metric                    | Target      | Measurement Period |
|---------------------------|-------------|-------------------|
| Infrastructure Uptime     | 99.95%      | Monthly           |
| Network Availability      | 99.99%      | Monthly           |
| Database Uptime           | 99.9%       | Monthly           |
| CDN Cache Hit Ratio       | ≥ 95%       | Weekly            |
| Incident Response (P1)    | ≤ 15 min    | Per incident      |
| Incident Response (P2)    | ≤ 1 hour    | Per incident      |
| Incident Resolution (P1)  | ≤ 4 hours   | Per incident      |
| Incident Resolution (P2)  | ≤ 8 hours   | Per incident      |
| Backup RPO                | ≤ 1 hour    | Continuous        |
| Backup RTO                | ≤ 2 hours   | Per incident      |

3. SERVICE CREDITS
If Provider fails to meet the uptime targets:
- 99.9% - 99.95%: 5% credit on monthly fee
- 99.5% - 99.9%: 15% credit on monthly fee
- Below 99.5%: 30% credit on monthly fee
- Below 99.0%: Client may terminate without penalty

4. FEES
Monthly service fee: £18,500 + VAT
Payment terms: Net 30 days
Annual review: fee adjustment capped at CPI + 2%

5. TERM AND TERMINATION
Initial term: 24 months from Effective Date
Renewal: Automatic 12-month renewal unless 90 days written notice
Early termination by Client: 6 months' fees as exit charge

6. ESCALATION
Level 1: NOC Team → Level 2: Service Delivery Manager (James Wright, j.wright@cloudpeak.co.uk) → Level 3: VP Operations (Sarah Chen)

Signed:
For CloudPeak: James Wright, Service Delivery Director
For Nordic Logistics: Erik Johansen, CTO
Date: March 25, 2026

input:
NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 20, 2026

Between:
Quantum AI Research Inc.
2500 Sand Hill Road, Suite 300, Menlo Park, CA 94025
("Disclosing Party")

And:
Berlin Neural Systems GmbH
Friedrichstraße 123, 10117 Berlin, Germany
HRB 234567 B
("Receiving Party")

1. PURPOSE
The parties wish to explore a potential collaboration in the field of neuromorphic computing hardware and wish to share certain proprietary and confidential information.

2. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All technical, business, financial, and strategic information disclosed by either party, including but not limited to: source code, algorithms, model architectures, training data specifications, chip designs, customer lists, pricing models, business plans, and patent applications.

3. OBLIGATIONS
The Receiving Party shall:
a) Use Confidential Information solely for evaluating the potential collaboration
b) Restrict access to employees and advisors with a need-to-know basis
c) Apply at least the same degree of care as for its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care
d) Not reverse-engineer any prototypes or samples shared
e) Not file any patent applications based on disclosed information

4. EXCLUSIONS
Information that: (a) is publicly available, (b) was known prior to disclosure, (c) is independently developed, (d) is disclosed by a third party without restriction.

5. TERM
This Agreement is effective for 3 years from the date of execution. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after disclosure of each piece of information.

6. RETURN OF MATERIALS
Upon termination or request, the Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information within 30 days and provide written certification of destruction.

7. REMEDIES
The Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other legal remedies. The Receiving Party acknowledges that breach may cause irreparable harm.

8. GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, USA. Disputes shall be resolved by binding arbitration under ICC rules in New York, NY.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF:
For Quantum AI Research Inc.: Dr. Lisa Wang, CEO
For Berlin Neural Systems GmbH: Prof. Dr. Thomas Müller, Geschäftsführer
Date: March 20, 2026
output:

SCOPE: Source code, algorithms, model architectures, training data specs, chip designs, customer lists, pricing, business plans, patent applications
OBLIGATIONS: Use only for evaluation, need-to-know access, no reverse-engineering, no patent filings based on disclosed info
EXCLUSIONS: Public info, prior knowledge, independent development, unrestricted third-party disclosure
TERM: 3 years; confidentiality survives 5 years per disclosure
RETURN: 30 days after termination + written certification of destruction
REMEDIES: Injunctive relief + legal remedies; breach = irreparable harm acknowledged
GOVERNING_LAW: Delaware, USA; ICC arbitration in New York

CONTRAT DE PRESTATION DE SERVICES

N° du contrat : CPS-2026-0198
Date : 18 mars 2026

Entre :
Agence Digitale LuminaWeb SARL
25 Rue du Faubourg Saint-Honoré, 75008 Paris
SIRET : 823 456 789 00015
Représentée par : Mme Claire Dubois, Gérante
(ci-après « le Prestataire »)

Et :
Maison Bellecour SAS
14 Place Bellecour, 69002 Lyon
SIRET : 912 345 678 00023
Représentée par : M. Antoine Moreau, Directeur Général
(ci-après « le Client »)

Article 1 — Objet
Le Prestataire s'engage à concevoir, développer et déployer un site e-commerce sur mesure pour la marque Maison Bellecour, incluant :
a) Design UX/UI responsive (mobile-first)
b) Développement front-end et back-end (Next.js + Medusa)
c) Intégration passerelle de paiement (Stripe + PayPal)
d) Intégration ERP existant (SAP Business One)
e) Migration des données produits (catalogue de 2 400 références)
f) Formation de l'équipe interne (2 jours sur site)

Article 2 — Calendrier
Phase 1 — Maquettes et validation UX : 01.04.2026 — 30.04.2026
Phase 2 — Développement : 01.05.2026 — 31.07.2026
Phase 3 — Intégrations et tests : 01.08.2026 — 31.08.2026
Phase 4 — Mise en production et formation : 01.09.2026 — 15.09.2026

Article 3 — Prix
Montant total HT : 92 000 EUR
Paiement en 4 échéances :
- 23 000 EUR à la signature
- 23 000 EUR à la livraison Phase 1
- 23 000 EUR à la livraison Phase 2
- 23 000 EUR à la mise en production
TVA : 20%
Délai de paiement : 30 jours date de facture

Article 4 — Propriété intellectuelle
Le Client devient propriétaire de l'ensemble des droits patrimoniaux sur les livrables dès paiement intégral. Le Prestataire conserve le droit de mentionner la réalisation dans son portfolio.

Article 5 — Pénalités de retard
En cas de retard du Prestataire : 0,5% du montant de la phase concernée par jour de retard, plafonné à 10%.
En cas de retard de paiement du Client : intérêts au taux légal majoré de 5 points + indemnité forfaitaire de 40 EUR.

Article 6 — Résiliation
Chaque partie peut résilier avec un préavis de 30 jours en cas de manquement grave non remédié dans les 15 jours suivant mise en demeure.

Article 7 — Loi applicable
Droit français. Tribunal compétent : Tribunal de Commerce de Paris.

Signatures :
Claire Dubois — LuminaWeb SARL
Antoine Moreau — Maison Bellecour SAS

1. WELKE GEGEVENS VERZAMELEN WIJ

1.5 Marketingvoorkeuren: nieuwsbriefinschrijving, opt-in/opt-out status

2. RECHTSGRONDEN
- Uitvoering overeenkomst (art. 6 lid 1 sub b AVG): verwerking bestel- en accountgegevens
- Gerechtvaardigd belang (art. 6 lid 1 sub f AVG): fraudepreventie, analytics
- Toestemming (art. 6 lid 1 sub a AVG): marketingcommunicatie, niet-essentiële cookies
- Wettelijke verplichting (art. 6 lid 1 sub c AVG): fiscale bewaarplicht (7 jaar)

- Accountgegevens: tot 2 jaar na laatste activiteit
- Bestelgegevens: 7 jaar (wettelijke bewaarplicht)

- Communicatiegegevens: 3 jaar
- Marketingvoorkeuren: tot intrekking toestemming

4. DELEN MET DERDEN
- Betalingsverwerker: Adyen N.V. (Amsterdam)
- Bezorgdiensten: PostNL, DHL
- E-mailmarketing: Mailchimp (VS — EU-VS Data Privacy Framework)
- Hosting: AWS EU (Frankfurt)
- Analytics: Matomo (self-hosted, geen gegevensoverdracht)

5. RECHTEN VAN BETROKKENEN
U heeft recht op: inzage, rectificatie, verwijdering, beperking, overdraagbaarheid, bezwaar.
Indienen via: privacy@brightcommerce.nl of schriftelijk. Reactietermijn: 30 dagen.
Klachten: Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens (www.autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl)

6. BEVEILIGING
TLS 1.3, AES-256 encryptie at-rest, MFA voor medewerkers, jaarlijkse pentest, ISO 27001-gecertificeerd.

7. COOKIES
Essentieel: altijd actief. Functioneel en analytisch: opt-in via cookiebanner. Marketing: alleen bij expliciete toestemming.

8. WIJZIGINGEN
Wij informeren klanten 30 dagen vooraf per e-mail bij materiële wijzigingen.

RELATÓRIO DE AUDITORIA FINANCEIRA

Cliente: Grupo Solar Energia S.A.
CNPJ: 12.345.678/0001-90
Sede: Avenida Paulista 1578, 14° andar, São Paulo — SP, CEP 01310-200

Auditor Independente: PKB Auditores Associados S/S
CRC-SP: 2SP034567/O-8
Responsável Técnico: CPA Dr. Rafael Mendes, CRC 1SP234567/O-3

Período auditado: Exercício fiscal encerrado em 31.12.2025
Data do relatório: 15.03.2026

1. OPINIÃO
Em nossa opinião, as demonstrações financeiras apresentam adequadamente, em todos os aspectos relevantes, a posição patrimonial e financeira do Grupo Solar Energia S.A. em 31 de dezembro de 2025, em conformidade com as práticas contábeis adotadas no Brasil (BR GAAP) e as normas internacionais (IFRS).

Opinião: SEM RESSALVAS

2. PRINCIPAIS INDICADORES FINANCEIROS
- Receita líquida: R$ 487.200.000
- EBITDA: R$ 98.400.000 (margem EBITDA: 20,2%)
- Lucro líquido: R$ 52.300.000 (margem líquida: 10,7%)
- Ativo total: R$ 1.234.500.000
- Patrimônio líquido: R$ 456.700.000
- Dívida líquida: R$ 312.800.000
- Dívida líquida/EBITDA: 3,18x
- ROE: 11,5%
- Liquidez corrente: 1,42

3. PONTOS DE ATENÇÃO
a) Concentração de receita: 42% da receita proveniente de 3 clientes — risco de dependência comercial
b) Aumento de 18% nas despesas operacionais, acima do crescimento da receita (12%)
c) Contingências trabalhistas estimadas em R$ 8.200.000 (provisionadas em R$ 5.100.000) — recomenda-se revisão
d) Prazo médio de recebimento aumentou de 45 para 58 dias — possível deterioração da qualidade do crédito

4. RECOMENDAÇÕES
1. Diversificar base de clientes para reduzir concentração de receita
2. Implementar programa de controle de custos operacionais
3. Revisar provisão para contingências trabalhistas com assessoria jurídica
4. Fortalecer política de crédito e cobrança
5. Avaliar renegociação de dívida para reduzir alavancagem (meta: abaixo de 2,5x EBITDA)

5. CONFORMIDADE
- Demonstrações em conformidade com CPC/IFRS
- Controles internos adequados, sem deficiências significativas identificadas
- Transações com partes relacionadas divulgadas nas notas explicativas

Dr. Rafael Mendes
Sócio Responsável — PKB Auditores Associados
output:

PROBLEMAS: Concentração de receita (42% em 3 clientes), despesas operacionais +18% vs receita +12%, contingências trabalhistas subprovisionadas (R$ 8,2M estimado vs R$ 5,1M provisionado), prazo recebimento 45→58 dias

Każda ze stron może wypowiedzieć umowę z 6-miesięcznym okresem wypowiedzenia. Wypowiedzenie ze skutkiem natychmiastowym w przypadku istotnego naruszenia warunków umowy.

COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT

This Agreement is entered into on March 10, 2026, by and between:

Landlord: Sterling Properties LLC, registered at 1200 Park Avenue, Suite 400, New York, NY 10128, EIN 13-4567890, represented by David R. Harrison, Managing Director

Tenant: BlueWave Analytics Inc., registered at 88 Pine Street, Floor 12, New York, NY 10005, EIN 20-9876543, represented by Sarah Chen, CEO

1. PREMISES
The Landlord hereby leases to the Tenant office space located at 350 Fifth Avenue, Floor 22, Suite 2201-2205, New York, NY 10118, comprising approximately 8,500 sq ft of usable space.

2. TERM
The lease term shall be five (5) years, commencing on May 1, 2026 and terminating on April 30, 2031, with an option to renew for two (2) additional periods of three (3) years each.

3. RENT
Base rent: $52.00 per sq ft per annum ($442,000/year), payable monthly at $36,833.33. Rent escalation: 3% annually beginning Year 2. Security deposit: $110,500 (3 months' rent).

4. COMMON AREA MAINTENANCE (CAM)
Tenant shall pay a proportionate share (4.2%) of building operating expenses, estimated at $8.50/sq ft/year ($72,250/year).

5. TENANT IMPROVEMENTS
Landlord provides a tenant improvement allowance of $45/sq ft ($382,500) for initial buildout. Any costs exceeding this allowance are the Tenant's responsibility.

6. PERMITTED USE
The Premises shall be used exclusively for general office, technology development, and data analytics operations.

7. INSURANCE
Tenant shall maintain commercial general liability insurance with minimum coverage of $2,000,000 per occurrence and $5,000,000 aggregate.

8. DEFAULT
Failure to pay rent within 10 days of due date constitutes default. Landlord may terminate with 30 days' written notice after default.

9. GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF:
David R. Harrison — Sterling Properties LLC
Sarah Chen — BlueWave Analytics Inc.

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

Effective Date: March 1, 2026

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into by and between:

Disclosing Party: Quantum Dynamics Corp., 2500 Sand Hill Road, Menlo Park, CA 94025, represented by Dr. James Liu, VP of Research

Receiving Party: Apex Engineering Solutions Ltd., 47 Innovation Way, Cambridge CB2 1TN, United Kingdom, represented by Helen Marshall, Director of Partnerships

1. PURPOSE
The parties wish to explore a potential collaboration in the field of quantum computing error correction (the "Purpose"). In connection with the Purpose, the Disclosing Party may share proprietary and confidential information with the Receiving Party.

2. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All technical data, trade secrets, algorithms, research findings, prototypes, source code, business plans, financial projections, customer lists, and any other information marked as "Confidential" or reasonably understood to be confidential.

3. OBLIGATIONS
The Receiving Party shall: (a) maintain confidentiality using at least the same degree of care as for its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care; (b) not disclose to third parties without prior written consent; (c) limit access to employees with a need to know who are bound by similar obligations; (d) not use the information for any purpose other than the Purpose.

4. EXCLUSIONS
Information that: (a) is publicly available; (b) was known prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed; (d) is disclosed pursuant to legal requirement with prompt notice.

5. TERM
This Agreement shall remain in effect for three (3) years from the Effective Date. Confidentiality obligations survive for five (5) years after disclosure of each item of Confidential Information.

6. REMEDIES
The Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity.

7. GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California.

Dr. James Liu — Quantum Dynamics Corp.
Helen Marshall — Apex Engineering Solutions Ltd.

CONTRAT DE PRESTATION DE SERVICES

Contrat N° : CPS-2026-0312
Fait à Paris, le 12 mars 2026

Entre les soussignés :

Le Prestataire :
DigiConsult SAS, SIREN 823 456 789, siège social : 15 rue de Rivoli, 75001 Paris
représentée par M. Antoine Lefèvre, Directeur Général

Le Client :
Groupe Alimentaire Dupont SA, SIREN 567 890 123, siège social : 42 avenue des Champs-Élysées, 75008 Paris
représenté par Mme Claire Moreau, Directrice des Systèmes d'Information

Article 1 — Objet
Le Prestataire s'engage à réaliser pour le Client une mission d'audit et de refonte du système d'information logistique, comprenant : audit de l'existant, rédaction du cahier des charges, développement de la solution, recette et mise en production.

Article 2 — Durée
La mission débute le 01.04.2026 et se termine au plus tard le 30.09.2026 (6 mois). Prolongation possible par avenant écrit.

Article 3 — Rémunération
Honoraires : 185 000 EUR HT, répartis comme suit :
- Phase 1 (Audit) : 35 000 EUR — facturation à la livraison du rapport
- Phase 2 (Développement) : 120 000 EUR — facturation mensuelle au prorata de l'avancement
- Phase 3 (Recette/MEP) : 30 000 EUR — facturation à la recette définitive
TVA applicable : 20%
Paiement à 30 jours fin de mois.

Article 4 — Pénalités de retard
Retard du Prestataire : 0,5% du montant de la phase par semaine de retard, plafonné à 10%. Retard de paiement du Client : intérêts au taux légal majoré de 3 points.

Article 5 — Propriété intellectuelle
Les développements réalisés dans le cadre de la mission deviennent la propriété exclusive du Client à compter du paiement intégral.

Article 6 — Confidentialité
Les parties s'engagent à maintenir la confidentialité des informations échangées pendant 3 ans après la fin du contrat.

Article 7 — Résiliation
Résiliation possible avec préavis de 2 mois. Résiliation immédiate en cas de manquement grave non corrigé sous 15 jours après mise en demeure.

Article 8 — Droit applicable et juridiction
Droit français. Tribunal de commerce de Paris.

Signatures :
Antoine Lefèvre — DigiConsult SAS
Claire Moreau — Groupe Alimentaire Dupont SA

Cláusula 1.ª — Objeto
O Prestador compromete-se a desenvolver e implementar um sistema integrado de gestão hospitalar (HIS — Hospital Information System), incluindo: módulo de registo clínico eletrónico, gestão de internamentos, farmácia hospitalar, laboratório e imagiologia, integração com o sistema nacional de saúde (SNS).

Cláusula 2.ª — Prazo
O contrato tem início em 01.05.2026 e termo em 30.04.2027 (12 meses). O prazo poderá ser prorrogado por acordo escrito das partes.

Cláusula 3.ª — Remuneração
Valor total: 420.000 EUR + IVA (23%), distribuído por fases:
- Fase 1 — Análise e desenho: 65.000 EUR (faturação na entrega do relatório)
- Fase 2 — Desenvolvimento: 230.000 EUR (faturação mensal proporcional)
- Fase 3 — Testes e formação: 75.000 EUR (faturação após aceitação)
- Fase 4 — Go-live e suporte: 50.000 EUR (faturação mensal, 6 meses pós go-live)
Pagamento a 30 dias, IBAN: PT50 0035 0786 0000 3456 7890 5 (Caixa Geral de Depósitos)

Cláusula 4.ª — Penalidades
Atraso na entrega de cada fase: 0,3% do valor da fase por dia de atraso, até ao máximo de 8%. Atraso no pagamento: juros de mora à taxa legal.

Cláusula 5.ª — Propriedade intelectual
O código-fonte e toda a documentação técnica são propriedade exclusiva do Cliente após pagamento integral.

Cláusula 6.ª — Confidencialidade
As partes obrigam-se a manter sigilo sobre todos os dados clínicos e técnicos, em conformidade com o RGPD (Regulamento 2016/679) e a Lei n.º 58/2019.

Cláusula 7.ª — Resolução
Resolução com pré-aviso de 90 dias. Resolução imediata por incumprimento grave não sanado em 30 dias após notificação.

Cláusula 8.ª — Foro
Tribunal da Comarca de Lisboa.

Dra. Ana Ferreira — Hospital Central do Tejo, S.A.

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 12, 2026 ("Effective Date")

BETWEEN:
NexGen Robotics Inc., a Delaware corporation, with principal offices at 2400 Sand Hill Road, Menlo Park, CA 94025 ("Disclosing Party")

AND:
Quantum Dynamics Ltd., incorporated in England and Wales, registered office at 78 Cavendish Square, London W1G 0AX, Company No. 09876543 ("Receiving Party")

1. PURPOSE: The parties wish to explore a potential strategic partnership involving autonomous drone navigation technology ("Purpose"). In connection therewith, the Disclosing Party may share certain proprietary and confidential information.

2. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION includes, but is not limited to: technical specifications, source code, algorithms, hardware designs, patent applications, business plans, financial projections, customer lists, and pricing strategies related to the Purpose.

3. OBLIGATIONS: The Receiving Party shall: (a) hold Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent; (c) use it solely for the Purpose; (d) limit internal access to employees with a need-to-know basis who are bound by similar obligations.

4. EXCLUSIONS: Information that: (a) was publicly available at time of disclosure; (b) becomes public through no fault of Receiving Party; (c) was independently developed; (d) was lawfully obtained from a third party; (e) is required to be disclosed by law or court order (with prompt notice to Disclosing Party).

5. TERM: This Agreement is effective for 3 years from the Effective Date. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after disclosure of each piece of Confidential Information.

6. REMEDIES: The Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity. The Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages may be inadequate.

7. GOVERNING LAW: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California, USA.

8. RETURN OF MATERIALS: Upon termination or request, Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information within 30 days and certify destruction in writing.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF:

NexGen Robotics Inc.
By: Sarah Chen, VP of Business Development
Date: March 12, 2026

Quantum Dynamics Ltd.
By: James Whitworth, Director of Strategic Partnerships
Date: March 12, 2026

- Revenue: £142.8M (Q4 2024: £118.3M, +20.7% YoY)

- Net Debt: £89.5M (down from £112.3M, leverage ratio 0.9x)

- Onshore Wind: £58.2M (40.8%) — 12 operational farms, 890MW installed capacity

- Solar: £31.5M (22.1%) — 28 sites across UK and Spain

- Regulatory uncertainty: UK Electricity Market Reform consultation ongoing

CONTRAT DE PRESTATION DE SERVICES

N° du contrat : CPS-2026-PAR-0156
Fait à Paris, le 10 mars 2026

ENTRE :
DigiConsult SAS
42 Avenue des Champs-Élysées, 75008 Paris
SIRET : 812 345 678 00012 | TVA : FR76812345678
Représentée par : Marc Dubois, Directeur Général
ci-après dénommée « le Prestataire »

ET :
LuxeRetail SA
15 Rue du Faubourg Saint-Honoré, 75008 Paris
SIRET : 798 765 432 00045 | TVA : FR41798765432
Représentée par : Claire Fontaine, Directrice des Opérations
ci-après dénommée « le Client »

Article 1 — OBJET
Le Prestataire s'engage à réaliser pour le Client une mission d'audit et de transformation digitale du système de gestion des stocks et de la chaîne logistique.

Article 2 — PRESTATIONS
- Phase 1 (avril-mai 2026) : Audit de l'existant et cartographie des flux
- Phase 2 (juin-août 2026) : Conception de la solution cible et cahier des charges
- Phase 3 (sept-déc 2026) : Développement et intégration (ERP Odoo, WMS, connecteurs e-commerce)
- Phase 4 (janv 2027) : Recette, formation et mise en production

Article 3 — PRIX
Montant total : 245 000 € HT
- Phase 1 : 35 000 € HT
- Phase 2 : 55 000 € HT
- Phase 3 : 120 000 € HT
- Phase 4 : 35 000 € HT
TVA applicable : 20 %
Paiement : 30 % à la commande, 40 % à la livraison Phase 2, 30 % à la recette finale
Délai de paiement : 30 jours date de facture

Article 4 — DURÉE
Du 1er avril 2026 au 31 janvier 2027 (10 mois).

Article 5 — PÉNALITÉS DE RETARD
0,5 % du montant de la phase concernée par semaine de retard, plafonné à 10 %.

Article 6 — CONFIDENTIALITÉ
Les parties s'engagent à maintenir la confidentialité des informations échangées pendant 3 ans après la fin du contrat.

Article 7 — PROPRIÉTÉ INTELLECTUELLE
Les développements réalisés dans le cadre du contrat sont la propriété exclusive du Client après paiement intégral.

Article 8 — RÉSILIATION
Résiliation possible par chaque partie avec un préavis de 60 jours. En cas de manquement grave, résiliation immédiate après mise en demeure restée infructueuse pendant 15 jours.

Article 9 — DROIT APPLICABLE
Droit français. Tribunal compétent : Tribunal de Commerce de Paris.

Signatures :
Marc Dubois — DigiConsult SAS
Claire Fontaine — LuxeRetail SA

Zamówienia przyjmowane 24/7 przez stronę internetową. Potwierdzenie zamówienia wysyłane na e-mail w ciągu 1h. Zamówienie realizowane po zaksięgowaniu płatności.

Ceny podane w PLN brutto (z VAT). Formy płatności: BLIK, karta płatnicza (Visa, Mastercard), przelew bankowy, PayPo (płatność odroczona do 30 dni). Minimalná wartość zamówienia: brak.
