COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT

This Commercial Lease Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 1, 2026, by and between:

Landlord: Riverside Properties LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, with principal offices at 500 Commerce Street, Suite 400, Nashville, TN 37203. EIN: 62-7654321.

Tenant: Bright Dental Group P.C., a Tennessee professional corporation, with registered offices at 211 Church Street, Nashville, TN 37201. EIN: 62-1234567.

1. PREMISES
The Landlord hereby leases to Tenant the commercial space located at 500 Commerce Street, Suite 210-212, Nashville, TN 37203, comprising approximately 2,800 square feet of office/clinic space on the second floor.

2. TERM
Initial term: 5 years, commencing April 1, 2026 through March 31, 2031.
Option to renew: Two (2) additional 3-year terms upon written notice 180 days prior to expiration.

3. RENT
Base rent: $6,200/month for Year 1, with annual escalation of 3%.
Triple net (NNN): Tenant pays proportional share of property taxes, insurance, and CAM (estimated $1,450/month).
Total estimated monthly cost Year 1: $7,650.
Rent due on the 1st of each month. Late fee: 5% if not received by the 10th.

4. SECURITY DEPOSIT
$18,600 (three months' base rent), held in escrow at First Tennessee Bank.

5. TENANT IMPROVEMENTS
Landlord provides a TI allowance of $45/sq ft ($126,000 total) for buildout of dental operatories. Work to be completed by Tenant's contractor, subject to Landlord approval of plans. Unused TI allowance is forfeited.

6. USE
Premises shall be used exclusively for dental practice and related healthcare services. No subletting without prior written consent.

7. INSURANCE
Tenant to maintain commercial general liability ($1M per occurrence, $2M aggregate) and professional liability insurance. Landlord to be named as additional insured.

8. TERMINATION
Early termination permitted after Year 3 with 12 months' written notice and payment of termination fee equal to 6 months' base rent at then-current rate.

Governing law: State of Tennessee.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first written above.

Riverside Properties LLC — By: James Harmon, Managing Member
Bright Dental Group P.C. — By: Dr. Sarah Chen, President
output:

TERM: 5 years (Apr 1, 2026 — Mar 31, 2031), two 3-year renewal options (180-day notice)

LATE_FEE: 5% if not paid by 10th

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

Effective Date: March 10, 2026

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("NDA") is entered into by and between:

Disclosing Party: Quantum Dynamics Inc., a California corporation, 1200 Innovation Way, Palo Alto, CA 94304. Contact: legal@quantumdynamics.io

Receiving Party: NovaBridge Consulting LLC, a New York limited liability company, 350 Fifth Avenue, Suite 3300, New York, NY 10118. Contact: contracts@novabridge.com

1. PURPOSE
The parties wish to explore a potential collaboration regarding quantum computing optimization services for financial modeling (the "Purpose"). In connection therewith, certain confidential information may be disclosed.

2. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All non-public, proprietary information disclosed by either party, including but not limited to: algorithms, source code, technical specifications, business plans, client lists, financial data, and trade secrets. Information marked "CONFIDENTIAL" or disclosed orally and confirmed in writing within 10 business days.

3. OBLIGATIONS
The Receiving Party shall: (a) hold Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not disclose to third parties without prior written consent; (c) limit access to employees/contractors with need-to-know who are bound by equivalent confidentiality obligations; (d) not use Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Purpose.

4. EXCLUSIONS
Obligations do not apply to information that: (a) is publicly available; (b) was known prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed; (d) is required by law or court order (with prompt notice to Disclosing Party).

5. TERM
This NDA is effective for 3 years from the Effective Date. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after disclosure of each piece of Confidential Information.

6. REMEDIES
Breach may cause irreparable harm. The Disclosing Party is entitled to injunctive relief in addition to any other remedies at law or equity.

7. RETURN OF MATERIALS
Upon termination or request, the Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information within 30 days and certify destruction in writing.

8. GOVERNING LAW
State of California. Disputes resolved by binding arbitration under JAMS rules, venue: San Francisco.

Quantum Dynamics Inc. — By: Dr. Michael Torres, CEO
NovaBridge Consulting LLC — By: Elena Vasquez, Managing Partner

Date: March 15, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to provide a professional reference for Ms. Rachel Thompson, who was employed at Meridian Analytics Ltd. from September 1, 2021 to February 28, 2026.

During her tenure, Ms. Thompson served in the following capacities:
- Data Analyst (Sep 2021 — Aug 2023)
- Senior Data Analyst (Sep 2023 — Mar 2025)
- Lead Data Scientist (Apr 2025 — Feb 2026)

In her most recent role as Lead Data Scientist, Ms. Thompson managed a team of 6 analysts and was responsible for:
- Designing and implementing predictive models for client churn analysis, achieving a 23% improvement in prediction accuracy
- Leading the migration of our analytics platform from on-premise to AWS (Redshift, SageMaker, QuickSight)
- Establishing automated reporting pipelines that reduced manual reporting time by 40 hours/month
- Presenting quarterly insights to C-level stakeholders across 12 enterprise clients

Ms. Thompson consistently exceeded performance targets, receiving "Exceeds Expectations" ratings in 4 out of 5 annual reviews. She was awarded Employee of the Year in 2024.

Her departure was voluntary; she left to pursue a doctoral program. We would enthusiastically rehire Ms. Thompson should the opportunity arise.

Please do not hesitate to contact me for further information.

Sincerely,
David Park
VP of Data & Analytics
Meridian Analytics Ltd.
45 Canary Wharf, London E14 5AB

+44 20 7946 0321

Article 1 — Objet
Le Prestataire s'engage à réaliser pour le Client la refonte complète du site e-commerce maisonlaurent.fr, incluant : design UX/UI, développement front-end et back-end, intégration du système de paiement Stripe, migration des données produits (catalogue de 2 400 références), et optimisation SEO.

Article 2 — Durée
La prestation débute le 01.04.2026 pour une durée de 4 mois. Livraison finale prévue le 31.07.2026.

L'ensemble des livrables devient la propriété du Client après paiement intégral. Le Prestataire conserve le droit de mentionner la réalisation dans son portfolio.

Chaque partie peut résilier avec un préavis de 30 jours. En cas de résiliation par le Client, les travaux réalisés sont facturés au prorata.

Droit français. Tribunal compétent : Tribunal de Commerce de Lyon.

Fait à Lyon, le 15.03.2026, en deux exemplaires originaux.

SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT (SLA)

Agreement No: SLA-2026-EU-0091
Effective Date: April 1, 2026

Between:
Nimbus Cloud Services Ltd.
200 Canary Wharf, Tower 3, London E14 5AB, UK
Company No: 09876543
("Provider")

and:

RetailMax Europe GmbH
Friedrichstraße 191, 10117 Berlin, Germany
HRB 234567
("Client")

1. SERVICES COVERED
Managed Kubernetes cluster hosting (3 production clusters, 2 staging), CDN (global edge network, 48 PoPs), DDoS mitigation (Layer 3-7), managed PostgreSQL (3 instances, daily backups).

2. SERVICE LEVELS
- Uptime Guarantee: 99.95% monthly (measured per service)
- Planned Maintenance: max 4 hours/month, with 72h advance notice
- Incident Response: P1 (critical) — 15 min, P2 (major) — 1h, P3 (minor) — 4h, P4 (low) — 24h
- RTO: 1 hour (P1), 4 hours (P2)
- RPO: 15 minutes (all production databases)

3. SERVICE CREDITS
If monthly uptime falls below 99.95%:
- 99.9% — 99.95%: 10% credit on monthly fee
- 99.0% — 99.9%: 25% credit
- Below 99.0%: 50% credit
Maximum credit cap: 50% of monthly invoice. Credits must be claimed within 30 days.

4. FEES
Monthly fee: €18,500 (excl. VAT). Annual commitment with auto-renewal. Invoiced on the 1st of each month, payment due within 14 days.

5. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate with 90 days written notice. Provider may terminate immediately for non-payment exceeding 60 days. Client may terminate immediately if uptime drops below 99.0% for 3 consecutive months.

6. GOVERNING LAW
This agreement is governed by English law. Disputes shall be resolved through arbitration under ICC Rules in London.

Signed:
James Whitfield, CTO — Nimbus Cloud Services Ltd.
Dr. Katharina Braun, VP Engineering — RetailMax Europe GmbH

The Parties wish to explore a potential collaboration on autonomous navigation systems for industrial drones (Project "Horizon"). In the course of discussions, Disclosing Party may share proprietary information.

2. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All technical data, trade secrets, algorithms, source code, hardware schematics, business plans, customer lists, pricing strategies, and any materials marked "Confidential" or reasonably understood to be confidential.

3. OBLIGATIONS
Receiving Party shall:
(a) Use Confidential Information solely for evaluating Project Horizon
(b) Restrict access to employees with a need-to-know basis (max 10 named individuals)
(c) Not reverse-engineer any prototypes or samples provided
(d) Return or destroy all materials within 14 days of written request

4. EXCLUSIONS
Information that: (a) is publicly available, (b) was already known, (c) is independently developed, (d) is disclosed by a third party without restriction.

5. TERM
This Agreement remains in effect for 3 years from the date of signing. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after expiration.

6. REMEDIES
Breach may cause irreparable harm. Disclosing Party is entitled to injunctive relief without bond, in addition to any other remedies. Liquidated damages for unauthorized disclosure: USD $500,000 per incident.

7. GOVERNING LAW
State of California, USA. Venue: Santa Clara County Superior Court.

Quantum Dynamics Inc. — Sarah Chen, VP of Business Development
Stellar Robotics Pty Ltd — David Okonkwo, Managing Director
output:

input:
REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL (RFP)

RFP Reference: RFP-2026-IT-0045
Issue Date: March 18, 2026
Submission Deadline: April 30, 2026, 5:00 PM GMT

Issuing Organization:
Northern Health Trust
Adelaide House, 39 Adelaide Street, Belfast BT2 8FD, Northern Ireland

Project: Digital Patient Portal Implementation

1. BACKGROUND
Northern Health Trust serves approximately 480,000 patients across 6 hospitals and 32 community facilities. We seek a vendor to design, develop, and deploy a patient-facing digital portal integrating with our existing Epic EHR system.

2. SCOPE OF WORK
- Patient registration and identity verification (NHS number integration)
- Appointment scheduling and management
- Access to medical records, test results, and discharge summaries
- Secure messaging with healthcare providers
- Prescription management and repeat ordering
- Integration with NHS App and GP Connect APIs
- Mobile app (iOS/Android) and responsive web portal
- Accessibility: WCAG 2.2 AA compliance
- Multi-language support (English, Irish, Polish, Mandarin)

3. TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
- Cloud-hosted (Azure preferred, NHS-approved)
- HL7 FHIR R4 interoperability
- ISO 27001 and NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit compliance
- 99.9% uptime SLA
- Support for 50,000 concurrent users

4. BUDGET
Estimated budget: £2.5M — £3.5M over 3 years (including implementation, licensing, and support).

5. EVALUATION CRITERIA
- Technical capability: 35%
- Experience in NHS/healthcare: 25%
- Cost: 20%
- Implementation timeline: 10%
- Innovation and future roadmap: 10%

6. SUBMISSION
Proposals must be submitted electronically to procurement@northernhealthtrust.nhs.uk
Queries: rfp-queries@northernhealthtrust.nhs.uk (deadline for queries: April 15, 2026)

Contact: Helen McAllister, Head of Digital Transformation
output:

REFERENCE: RFP-2026-IT-0045
ISSUER: Northern Health Trust, Belfast, Northern Ireland
ISSUE_DATE: March 18, 2026
DEADLINE: April 30, 2026, 5:00 PM GMT
PROJECT: Digital Patient Portal — integration with Epic EHR for 480,000 patients across 6 hospitals and 32 community facilities
SCOPE: Patient registration (NHS number), appointments, medical records access, secure messaging, prescriptions, NHS App/GP Connect integration, mobile apps (iOS/Android), web portal, WCAG 2.2 AA, multi-language (EN/Irish/Polish/Mandarin)
TECHNICAL: Azure (NHS-approved), HL7 FHIR R4, ISO 27001, NHS DSPT compliant, 99.9% uptime, 50,000 concurrent users
BUDGET: £2.5M — £3.5M over 3 years (implementation + licensing + support)
EVALUATION: Technical 35%, NHS experience 25%, Cost 20%, Timeline 10%, Innovation 10%
SUBMISSION: procurement@northernhealthtrust.nhs.uk
QUERIES: rfp-queries@northernhealthtrust.nhs.uk (deadline April 15, 2026)
CONTACT: Helen McAllister, Head of Digital Transformation

12 Rue du Faubourg Saint-Honoré, 75008 Paris
SIRET : 456 789 012 00034
Représentée par M. Antoine Laurent, Gérant
(ci-après « le Client »)

Article 1 — OBJET
Le Prestataire s'engage à réaliser la refonte complète du site e-commerce du Client, incluant : design UX/UI, développement front-end et back-end, intégration du système de paiement (Stripe, PayPal, Apple Pay), migration des données produits (catalogue de 2 400 articles), optimisation SEO, et formation de l'équipe (3 sessions de 2h).

Article 2 — DURÉE
Début des travaux : 1er avril 2026. Livraison finale : 31 juillet 2026 (4 mois). Phase de garantie : 3 mois après livraison.

Article 3 — PRIX
Montant total : 87 000 € HT.
Échéancier : 30% à la signature (26 100 €), 30% à la validation des maquettes, 30% à la livraison, 10% à la fin de la période de garantie.

Article 4 — PROPRIÉTÉ INTELLECTUELLE
L'ensemble des créations (code source, design, contenus) sera cédé au Client après paiement intégral.

Article 5 — PÉNALITÉS
Retard de livraison : 0,5% du montant total par semaine de retard, plafonné à 10%. Retard de paiement : intérêts de retard au taux légal majoré de 3 points + indemnité forfaitaire de 40 €.

Article 6 — RÉSILIATION
Chaque partie peut résilier avec un préavis de 30 jours. En cas de résiliation par le Client, les travaux réalisés sont facturés au prorata.

Article 7 — DROIT APPLICABLE
Droit français. Tribunal compétent : Tribunal de Commerce de Paris.

Signatures :
Claire Dubois — Prestataire
Antoine Laurent — Client

Ceny brutto (z VAT 23%). Formy płatności: przelew bankowy, BLIK, karta płatnicza (Visa/Mastercard), PayPo (odroczona płatność do 30 dni). Nie przyjmujemy płatności za pobraniem.

§4. Dostawa
Kurier DPD: 14,99 PLN (darmowa od 200 PLN). InPost Paczkomat: 11,99 PLN (darmowa od 150 PLN). Odbiór osobisty: bezpłatny (Łódź, ul. Piotrkowska 120).

§5. Zwroty i reklamacje
Prawo odstąpienia: 30 dni od odbioru (ustawowe 14 dni + dodatkowe 16 dni). Zwrot kosztów w ciągu 7 dni roboczych od otrzymania zwrotu. Reklamacja: rozpatrzenie do 14 dni kalendarzowych. Formularz zwrotu/reklamacji na stronie.

§6. Dane osobowe
Administrator: ModaPolska Sp. z o.o. Dane przetwarzane na podstawie art. 6 ust. 1 lit. b) i f) RODO. IOD: iod@modapolska.pl. Pełna polityka prywatności: modapolska.pl/prywatnosc.

§7. Postanowienia końcowe
Sąd właściwy: wg przepisów o ochronie konsumentów. Regulamin może być zmieniony z 14-dniowym wyprzedzeniem.

Between:
Provider: NorthCloud Infrastructure GmbH, Friedrichstraße 123, 10117 Berlin, Germany, VAT DE298765432
Client: Meridian Financial Services plc, 25 Canary Wharf, London E14 5AB, UK, VAT GB987654321

1. SERVICES COVERED
Managed Kubernetes cluster hosting (3 production clusters, 2 staging), Object storage (S3-compatible, 50TB allocation), CDN services (EU + NA PoPs), 24/7 monitoring and incident response.

2. SERVICE LEVELS
- Platform Uptime: 99.95% monthly (excludes scheduled maintenance, max 4h/month maintenance window: Sundays 02:00-06:00 CET)
- API Response Time: p95 < 200ms, p99 < 500ms
- Incident Response: P1 (service down) — 15 min; P2 (degraded) — 1h; P3 (minor) — 4h; P4 (cosmetic) — next business day
- Data Durability: 99.999999999% (11 nines) for object storage
- RTO: 4 hours; RPO: 1 hour

3. SERVICE CREDITS
Monthly uptime below 99.95%: 10% credit. Below 99.9%: 25% credit. Below 99.0%: 50% credit. Maximum credit: 50% of monthly fee. Credits must be claimed within 30 days.

4. FEES
Monthly fee: €18,500. Annual commitment: €222,000. Payment: NET 30 from invoice date.

5. TERM AND TERMINATION
Initial term: 24 months. Auto-renewal for 12-month periods. Termination: 90 days written notice before renewal. Immediate termination for material breach (30-day cure period).

6. ESCALATION
Level 1: Technical Account Manager (TAM). Level 2: VP Engineering. Level 3: CTO. Escalation within 2 hours if SLA breach ongoing.

Signed:
Dr. Klaus Weber — NorthCloud Infrastructure GmbH
Sarah Mitchell — Meridian Financial Services plc

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 20, 2026.

Between:
Disclosing Party: Quantum Innovations Inc., 500 Sand Hill Road, Suite 300, Menlo Park, CA 94025, USA
Receiving Party: Atlas Ventures LLP, 14 St. James's Square, London SW1Y 4LG, UK

WHEREAS the Disclosing Party possesses certain proprietary and confidential information relating to its quantum computing platform, chip architecture, and go-to-market strategy ("Confidential Information") and wishes to disclose same to the Receiving Party for the purpose of evaluating a potential Series C investment.

1. DEFINITION: Confidential Information includes all technical data, trade secrets, business plans, financial projections, customer lists, product roadmaps, source code, algorithms, and any information marked "Confidential" or reasonably understood to be confidential.

2. OBLIGATIONS: Receiving Party shall (a) hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not disclose to any third party without prior written consent; (c) use Confidential Information solely for evaluating the potential investment; (d) limit internal disclosure to partners and analysts with need-to-know, who are bound by equivalent obligations.

3. EXCLUSIONS: Information that (a) is publicly available; (b) was known prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed; (d) is disclosed pursuant to court order (with prompt notice to Disclosing Party).

4. TERM: This Agreement is effective for 3 years from the date of execution. Obligations of confidentiality survive for 5 years after disclosure of each item.

5. RETURN OF MATERIALS: Upon request or termination, Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information within 15 business days and certify destruction in writing.

6. REMEDIES: Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other legal remedies. Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages may be insufficient.

7. GOVERNING LAW: State of Delaware, USA. Disputes resolved by binding arbitration under ICC rules, venue: New York.

Quantum Innovations Inc. — Dr. Lisa Chen, CEO
Atlas Ventures LLP — James Whitfield, Managing Partner

CONTRAT DE PRESTATION DE SERVICES INFORMATIQUES

Date : 18 mars 2026

Entre :
Prestataire : CloudNine Technologies SAS, 42 Avenue des Champs-Élysées, 75008 Paris
SIRET : 823 456 789 00012, RCS Paris B 823 456 789
Représenté par : M. Philippe Durand, Directeur Général

Client : Groupe Médicis SA, 15 Rue de Rivoli, 75001 Paris
SIRET : 912 345 678 00034, RCS Paris B 912 345 678
Représenté par : Mme Claire Lefèvre, Directrice des Systèmes d'Information

Article 1 — Objet
Le Prestataire s'engage à fournir les services suivants : migration de l'infrastructure vers le cloud AWS (35 serveurs), mise en place d'un environnement CI/CD, formation des équipes internes (20 personnes, 5 jours).

Article 2 — Durée
Du 15 avril 2026 au 14 octobre 2026 (6 mois). Prolongation possible par avenant.

Article 3 — Prix
Montant forfaitaire : 185 000 € HT
- Phase 1 (audit + architecture) : 35 000 € — facturation à la signature
- Phase 2 (migration) : 110 000 € — facturation mensuelle au prorata
- Phase 3 (formation + documentation) : 40 000 € — facturation à la livraison
TVA : 20%. Paiement : 30 jours date de facture.

Article 4 — Pénalités
Retard de livraison : 0,5% du montant de la phase par semaine de retard, plafonnées à 10%. Retard de paiement : intérêts de retard au taux BCE + 10 points.

Article 5 — Propriété intellectuelle
Les livrables (scripts, documentation, configurations) deviennent propriété du Client à la livraison et au paiement intégral.

Article 6 — Confidentialité
Les parties s'engagent à la confidentialité pendant la durée du contrat et 3 ans après son terme.

Article 7 — Résiliation
Résiliation pour faute avec mise en demeure préalable de 30 jours. Résiliation pour convenance par le Client avec préavis de 60 jours et paiement des prestations réalisées.

Droit applicable : droit français. Tribunal compétent : Tribunal de Commerce de Paris.

Philippe Durand — CloudNine Technologies SAS
Claire Lefèvre — Groupe Médicis SA

CONTRATO DE PRESTAÇÃO DE SERVIÇOS DE CONSULTORIA

Contrato n.º: CSC-2026-LX-0045
Data: 19 de março de 2026

Entre:
Prestador: TechBridge Consultoria Lda., Avenida da Liberdade 245, 3º andar, 1250-143 Lisboa
NIPC: 516 234 567
Representado por: Eng.ª Ana Rodrigues, Sócia-Gerente

Cliente: Porto Digital S.A., Rua das Flores 100, 4050-262 Porto
NIPC: 509 876 543
Representado por: Dr. Miguel Santos, Administrador

Cláusula 1.ª — Objeto
O Prestador compromete-se a realizar consultoria em transformação digital, incluindo: auditoria dos processos atuais, definição de roadmap tecnológico (12 meses), seleção e implementação de ferramentas de automação (RPA), e acompanhamento pós-implementação.

Cláusula 2.ª — Prazo
De 1 de abril de 2026 a 30 de setembro de 2026 (6 meses). Renovação por períodos de 3 meses mediante acordo escrito.

Cláusula 3.ª — Preço
Valor global: 78.000 € (+ IVA 23%)
- Fase 1 — Auditoria: 18.000 € (abril)
- Fase 2 — Roadmap e seleção: 22.000 € (maio-junho)
- Fase 3 — Implementação RPA: 28.000 € (julho-agosto)
- Fase 4 — Acompanhamento: 10.000 € (setembro)
Pagamento: fatura mensal, vencimento a 30 dias.

Cláusula 4.ª — Obrigações do Prestador
Equipa dedicada: 1 consultor sénior (mín. 4 dias/semana), 1 consultor júnior. Relatórios quinzenais de progresso. Entrega de documentação final.

Cláusula 5.ª — Confidencialidade
As partes comprometem-se a manter sigilo sobre toda a informação trocada, durante e 2 anos após o termo do contrato.

Cláusula 6.ª — Penalizações
Atraso na entrega de fases: 1% do valor da fase por semana, máximo 8%. Incumprimento grave: rescisão com justa causa.

Cláusula 7.ª — Rescisão
Por qualquer das partes com aviso prévio de 60 dias. Em caso de rescisão, são devidos os serviços já prestados.

Lei aplicável: Lei portuguesa. Foro: Comarca de Lisboa.

Ana Rodrigues — TechBridge Consultoria Lda.
Miguel Santos — Porto Digital S.A.

DECYZJA NR DŚ.6220.15.2026

Burmistrz Miasta Otwock
ul. Armii Krajowej 5, 05-400 Otwock

Otwock, dnia 18.03.2026 r.

Na podstawie art. 71 ust. 1, art. 75 ust. 1 pkt 4, art. 84 ust. 1 ustawy z dnia 3 października 2008 r. o udostępnianiu informacji o środowisku i jego ochronie, udziale społeczeństwa w ochronie środowiska oraz o ocenach oddziaływania na środowisko (Dz.U. z 2025 r. poz. 1523 ze zm.), po rozpatrzeniu wniosku z dnia 10.01.2026 r.

ORZEKAM:

1. Stwierdzam brak potrzeby przeprowadzenia oceny oddziaływania na środowisko dla przedsięwzięcia pn.: "Budowa hali magazynowo-produkcyjnej z zapleczem biurowym przy ul. Karczewskiej 150, Otwock, dz. nr ewid. 45/12, 45/13 obręb 0042".

2. Inwestor: ProBuild Development Sp. z o.o., ul. Nowowiejska 10, 00-653 Warszawa, NIP 5252345678.

3. Charakterystyka przedsięwzięcia:
- Hala magazynowo-produkcyjna o pow. zabudowy 2 800 m², kubatura 22 400 m³
- Zaplecze biurowe 450 m² (2 kondygnacje)
- Parking: 45 miejsc, w tym 3 dla niepełnosprawnych
- Przyłącza: woda, kanalizacja, energia elektryczna, gaz
- Szacunkowy koszt inwestycji: 12 500 000 PLN

4. Decyzja ważna 6 lat od dnia uprawomocnienia.

UZASADNIENIE:
Przedsięwzięcie kwalifikuje się jako mogące potencjalnie znacząco oddziaływać na środowisko (§3 ust. 1 pkt 52 lit. b rozporządzenia Rady Ministrów z dnia 10 września 2019 r.). Po analizie karty informacyjnej przedsięwzięcia, opinii RDOŚ w Warszawie (pismo WOO-II.4220.89.2026 z 15.02.2026) oraz Państwowego Powiatowego Inspektora Sanitarnego (pismo ZNS.7121.12.2026 z 20.02.2026), organ stwierdził brak potrzeby przeprowadzenia oceny.

POUCZENIE:
Od niniejszej decyzji służy odwołanie do Samorządowego Kolegium Odwoławczego w Warszawie w terminie 14 dni od dnia doręczenia.

z up. Burmistrza
mgr inż. Tomasz Borkowski
Naczelnik Wydziału Ochrony Środowiska

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 12, 2026 ("Effective Date")

BETWEEN:
QuantumLeap AI Inc., a Delaware corporation, with principal offices at 2200 Sand Hill Road, Suite 300, Menlo Park, CA 94025, USA ("Disclosing Party")

AND:
Nordic Data Solutions AB, a Swedish corporation, reg. nr 556789-0123, with offices at Sveavägen 44, 111 34 Stockholm, Sweden ("Receiving Party")

1. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All non-public technical, business, financial, and strategic information disclosed by Disclosing Party, including but not limited to: proprietary algorithms, model architectures, training datasets, customer lists, pricing structures, product roadmaps, and source code.

2. OBLIGATIONS
The Receiving Party shall: (a) hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not disclose to third parties without prior written consent; (c) limit access to employees and contractors with a need-to-know basis who are bound by similar confidentiality obligations; (d) use Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a potential strategic partnership ("Purpose").

3. EXCLUSIONS
Information that: (a) is publicly available; (b) was known prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed; (d) is required by law or court order (with prompt notice to Disclosing Party).

4. TERM
This Agreement remains in effect for 3 years from the Effective Date. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after disclosure of each piece of Confidential Information.

5. REMEDIES
Breach may cause irreparable harm. Disclosing Party is entitled to injunctive relief in addition to other remedies. Liquidated damages: USD 500,000 per breach incident.

6. GOVERNING LAW
State of Delaware, USA. Disputes resolved by binding arbitration under ICC rules, venue: New York City.

7. RETURN OF MATERIALS
Upon termination or request, Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information within 30 days and certify destruction in writing.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF:

QuantumLeap AI Inc.
By: Dr. Sarah Chen, CEO
Date: March 12, 2026

Nordic Data Solutions AB
By: Erik Lindström, Managing Director
Date: March 12, 2026

input:
REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL

RFP Reference: RFP-GOV-2026-IT-0078
Issued by: Department of Digital Infrastructure, Government of Ireland
Address: 29-31 Adelaide Road, Dublin D02 X285

Date of Issue: March 10, 2026
Deadline for Submissions: April 25, 2026, 17:00 IST
Contact: procurement@digitalgov.ie

1. PROJECT OVERVIEW
The Department seeks proposals for the design, implementation, and maintenance of a National Digital Identity Platform ("DigiID Ireland") enabling citizens to securely authenticate with government services online.

2. SCOPE OF WORK
- Identity verification (document scanning, biometric matching, liveness detection)
- Multi-factor authentication (FIDO2/WebAuthn, SMS, authenticator app)
- eIDAS 2.0 compliance for EU cross-border recognition
- Integration with 14 government agency systems via REST APIs
- Mobile application (iOS + Android) and web portal
- Admin dashboard for identity lifecycle management

3. TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS
- Cloud-hosted within EU (AWS eu-west-1 or Azure West Europe)
- ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II certified infrastructure
- Support for 4.9 million potential users
- Peak load: 50,000 concurrent sessions
- Availability: 99.99%
- Data retention per GDPR and Irish Data Protection Act 2018

4. BUDGET
Estimated budget: EUR 8,000,000 – 12,000,000 (excl. VAT) over 5 years including 3 years of maintenance.

5. EVALUATION CRITERIA
- Technical approach and architecture: 40%
- Experience and references: 25%
- Price: 20%
- Implementation timeline: 15%

6. SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS
- Technical proposal (max 60 pages)
- Financial proposal (separate sealed document)
- Minimum 3 references of similar-scale identity platforms
- Proof of ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II
- Consortium bids permitted (identify lead contractor)

7. QUESTIONS
Submit questions by April 4, 2026 to procurement@digitalgov.ie. Answers published April 11, 2026.

Issued by:
Siobhán O'Brien
Head of Procurement
Department of Digital Infrastructure
output:

REFERENCE: RFP-GOV-2026-IT-0078

DEADLINE: April 25, 2026, 17:00 IST
PROJECT: National Digital Identity Platform (DigiID Ireland)
SCOPE: Identity verification (document/biometric/liveness), MFA (FIDO2/WebAuthn), eIDAS 2.0, integration with 14 agencies via REST, mobile apps (iOS/Android), web portal, admin dashboard
TECHNICAL: EU cloud (AWS eu-west-1 or Azure West Europe), ISO 27001 + SOC 2 Type II, 4.9M users, 50K concurrent sessions, 99.99% availability, GDPR + Irish DPA 2018
BUDGET: EUR 8–12M (excl. VAT) over 5 years (incl. 3 years maintenance)
EVALUATION: Technical 40%, experience 25%, price 20%, timeline 15%
SUBMISSION: Technical (max 60 pages) + financial (sealed), 3 references, ISO/SOC proof, consortia allowed
QUESTIONS: By April 4, 2026; answers April 11, 2026
CONTACT: procurement@digitalgov.ie

QUARTERLY FINANCIAL REPORT

Company: Evergreen Renewable Energy plc
LSE Ticker: ERE.L
Period: Q4 2025 (October 1 — December 31, 2025)
Report Date: March 14, 2026

KEY FINANCIALS:
- Revenue: £142.3M (Q4 2024: £118.7M, +19.9% YoY)
- Gross Profit: £48.6M (margin 34.2%, vs 31.8% Q4 2024)
- EBITDA: £31.4M (margin 22.1%, vs 18.9% Q4 2024)
- Net Income: £14.8M (vs £8.2M Q4 2024, +80.5%)
- EPS: £0.37 (vs £0.21)
- Free Cash Flow: £22.1M
- Net Debt: £89.4M (Net Debt/EBITDA: 0.71x)

OPERATIONAL HIGHLIGHTS:
- Commissioned 3 new onshore wind farms (total 180MW capacity) in Scotland and Wales
- Solar portfolio reached 420MW installed capacity (+65MW in Q4)
- Secured 12-year PPA with National Grid at £52/MWh for 200MW
- Battery storage pilot (50MWh) operational at Dundee site

SEGMENT BREAKDOWN:
| Segment        | Revenue  | EBITDA   | Margin |
|----------------|----------|----------|---------|
| Wind Onshore   | £78.2M   | £19.1M   | 24.4%  |
| Solar          | £41.5M   | £10.8M   | 26.0%  |
| Services       | £22.6M   | £1.5M    | 6.6%   |

OUTLOOK FY2026:
- Revenue guidance: £580M – £610M
- EBITDA guidance: £130M – £145M
- Capex plan: £95M (primarily offshore wind development)
- Dividend: interim £0.08/share confirmed for H1 2026

RISK FACTORS:
- Regulatory changes to UK renewable subsidy framework
- Supply chain constraints on wind turbine components
- Interest rate sensitivity on project financing

Approved by the Board of Directors
Chairman: Sir Robert Ashworth
CFO: Claire Henderson, FCCA

ALGEMENE VOORWAARDEN — WEBDESIGN EN HOSTING

Versie 3.2 — Geldig vanaf 1 maart 2026

Digicraft BV
Keizersgracht 412, 1016 GC Amsterdam
KvK: 76543210
BTW: NL862345678B01

Artikel 1 — Definities
1.1 "Opdrachtnemer": DigiCraft BV
1.2 "Opdrachtgever": de natuurlijke of rechtspersoon die een overeenkomst aangaat met Opdrachtnemer
1.3 "Diensten": webdesign, webontwikkeling, hosting, onderhoud en aanverwante diensten

Artikel 2 — Toepasselijkheid
Deze voorwaarden zijn van toepassing op alle aanbiedingen, offertes en overeenkomsten. Afwijkingen uitsluitend schriftelijk.

Artikel 3 — Offertes en prijzen
3.1 Offertes zijn 30 dagen geldig.
3.2 Prijzen zijn exclusief BTW (21%).
3.3 Jaarlijkse prijsindexatie conform CBS-consumentenprijsindex, met maximum van 5%.

Artikel 4 — Uitvoering
4.1 Levertijd webdesignproject: 6-12 weken na goedkeuring offerte.
4.2 Maximaal 3 revisierondes inbegrepen. Extra revisies: EUR 95/uur.
4.3 Opdrachtgever levert content (tekst, afbeeldingen) binnen 10 werkdagen na verzoek.

Artikel 5 — Hosting
5.1 Beschikbaarheid: 99,9% op jaarbasis (excl. gepland onderhoud).
5.2 Gepland onderhoud: maximaal 4 uur per maand, buiten kantooruren (22:00-06:00).
5.3 Dagelijkse backups, retentie 30 dagen.
5.4 SSL-certificaat inbegrepen.

Artikel 6 — Betaling
6.1 Webdesign: 40% bij opdracht, 40% bij oplevering concept, 20% bij livegang.
6.2 Hosting: jaarlijks vooruit, facturatie 30 dagen voor verlenging.
6.3 Betalingstermijn: 14 dagen na factuurdatum.
6.4 Verzuimrente: 2% per maand over openstaand bedrag.

Artikel 7 — Intellectueel eigendom
7.1 Eigendom van ontwerpen gaat over na volledige betaling.
7.2 Opdrachtnemer behoudt recht op naamsvermelding en portfoliogebruik.
7.3 Gebruikte open source componenten behouden hun oorspronkelijke licentie.

Artikel 8 — Aansprakelijkheid
8.1 Maximale aansprakelijkheid: bedrag van de betreffende opdracht, met maximum van EUR 25.000.
8.2 Geen aansprakelijkheid voor indirecte schade, gevolgschade of gederfde winst.

Artikel 9 — Opzegging
9.1 Hostingcontracten: opzegtermijn 3 maanden voor einde contractjaar.
9.2 Bij opzegging: data-export binnen 30 dagen na beëindiging.

Artikel 10 — Geschillen
Nederlands recht. Bevoegde rechter: Rechtbank Amsterdam.

DigiCraft BV — Directie

input:
USER: Dostałem taką reklamację od klienta. Co powinienem zrobić?

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
Od: marta.lewandowska@logispeed.pl
Do: reklamacje@transmag.pl
Temat: Uszkodzona przesyłka — zlecenie TR/2026/1187

Szanowni Państwo,

w dniu 25.03.2026 odebraliśmy przesyłkę w ramach zlecenia transportowego TR/2026/1187 z dnia 18.03.2026. Po otwarciu palet stwierdziliśmy uszkodzenia mechaniczne 12 z 48 kartonów z elektroniką użytkową (monitory LCD 27"). Wartość uszkodzonego towaru szacujemy na 38 400 PLN netto.

Zgodnie z §7 ust. 3 umowy ramowej UM/2025/034 przewoźnik ponosi pełną odpowiedzialność za uszkodzenia powstałe w transporcie. Załączam dokumentację fotograficzną (14 zdjęć) oraz protokół szkody nr PS/2026/089 podpisany przez kierowcę.

Żądamy:
1. Odszkodowania w kwocie 38 400 PLN netto w terminie 14 dni
2. Wyjaśnienia przyczyn uszkodzenia
3. Planu naprawczego na przyszłość

Brak odpowiedzi w ciągu 7 dni roboczych traktować będziemy jako odmowę i skierujemy sprawę do kancelarii prawnej.

Z poważaniem,
Marta Lewandowska
Kierownik Logistyki, LogiSpeed Sp. z o.o.
---END DOCUMENT---

ASSISTANT: To poważna reklamacja z mocnymi podstawami — mają protokół szkody podpisany przez kierowcę i dokumentację fotograficzną. Rekomendowałbym szybkie zbadanie okoliczności i przygotowanie odpowiedzi.
USER: Ale czy kierowca mógł podpisać protokół pod presją? Nie wiem czy to wiążące.
ASSISTANT: Protokół szkody podpisany przez kierowcę jest standardowym dokumentem w branży TSL i ma moc dowodową. Trudno będzie go podważyć, chyba że macie dowody na nieprawidłowości przy odbiorze.
USER: OK, masz rację. A co z kwotą — 38 400 za 12 monitorów to dużo. Jak to zweryfikować?
ASSISTANT: Możecie zażądać faktur zakupu za te monitory lub wyceny niezależnego rzeczoznawcy. 38 400 za 12 monitorów 27" to 3 200 PLN/szt. netto — kwota realistyczna dla monitorów klasy biznesowej.
USER: Faktycznie, cena jest realna. Przygotuj projekt odpowiedzi — przyznajemy odpowiedzialność, ale proponujemy rozłożenie na 2 raty.
output:
TEMAT: Reklamacja uszkodzenia przesyłki w transporcie — monitory LCD
DOKUMENT_ŹRÓDŁOWY: Mail od Marty Lewandowskiej (LogiSpeed Sp. z o.o.) do reklamacje@transmag.pl
KLUCZOWE_FAKTY_Z_DOKUMENTU:
- Zlecenie: TR/2026/1187 z 18.03.2026, odbiór 25.03.2026
- Uszkodzenie: 12 z 48 kartonów, monitory LCD 27"
- Wartość szkody: 38 400 PLN netto
- Podstawa: umowa ramowa UM/2025/034, §7 ust. 3
- Dowody: 14 zdjęć + protokół szkody PS/2026/089 (podpisany przez kierowcę)
- Żądania: odszkodowanie 14 dni, wyjaśnienie przyczyn, plan naprawczy
- Termin odpowiedzi: 7 dni roboczych
USTALENIA_Z_ROZMOWY:
- Protokół podpisany przez kierowcę ma moc dowodową, trudny do podważenia
- Kwota 3 200 PLN/szt. netto jest realistyczna dla monitorów biznesowych
- Firma przyznaje odpowiedzialność
FEEDBACK_UŻYTKOWNIKA:
- "Ale czy kierowca mógł podpisać protokół pod presją?" (wątpliwość — rozwiana przez AI)
- "Faktycznie, cena jest realna" (potwierdzenie analizy AI)
ACTION_ITEMS:
- Przygotować odpowiedź przyznającą odpowiedzialność
- Zaproponować rozłożenie odszkodowania na 2 raty
DECYZJE: Uznanie reklamacji, propozycja płatności w 2 ratach

input:
USER: Mam dwie oferty na system ERP. Porównaj je.

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
OFERTA A — SoftHouse Sp. z o.o.
System: SoftERP Pro 2026
Licencja: 120 000 PLN (jednorazowa, 50 stanowisk)
Wdrożenie: 80 000 PLN (szacowane 400 roboczogodzin)
Utrzymanie roczne: 18 000 PLN (aktualizacje + support 8-16)
Moduły: FK, Kadry-Płace, Magazyn, CRM, BI
Technologia: .NET 8 + MSSQL, hosting on-premise
SLA: 99.9%, czas reakcji 4h (dni robocze)
Gwarancja: 24 miesiące od odbioru
---END DOCUMENT---

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
OFERTA B — CloudWorks S.A.
System: CloudERP Enterprise
Licencja: SaaS — 2 800 PLN/miesiąc (do 60 stanowisk)
Wdrożenie: 45 000 PLN (szacowane 250 roboczogodzin)
Utrzymanie: wliczone w subskrypcję
Moduły: FK, Kadry-Płace, Magazyn, CRM, BI, e-Commerce
Technologia: Cloud-native (AWS), API REST
SLA: 99.95%, czas reakcji 1h (24/7)
Gwarancja: ciągłe aktualizacje w ramach subskrypcji
---END DOCUMENT---

ASSISTANT: Obie oferty mają podobny zakres modułów, ale różnią się modelem kosztów i technologią. Oferta A to rozwiązanie on-premise z jednorazową licencją, oferta B to SaaS z subskrypcją miesięczną. W perspektywie 3 lat koszt oferty B wyniesie ok. 145 800 PLN (wdrożenie + 36 × 2 800), podczas gdy oferta A to ok. 254 000 PLN (licencja + wdrożenie + 3 × utrzymanie).
USER: Źle liczysz ofertę A. 120k + 80k + 3×18k = 254k, to się zgadza. Ale w ofercie B nie uwzględniłeś że po 3 latach w A mamy system na własność, a w B dalej płacimy.
ASSISTANT: Słuszna uwaga. W modelu TCO po 5 latach: oferta A = 290 000 PLN (200k + 5×18k), oferta B = 213 000 PLN (45k + 60×2 800). Ale po 5 latach rezygnacja z B oznacza utratę dostępu, w A system pozostaje.
USER: Dokładnie. A co z SLA? 99.95% u B jest lepsze.
ASSISTANT: Tak, SLA 99.95% z reakcją 1h 24/7 jest zdecydowanie lepsze niż 99.9% z reakcją 4h w dni robocze. Jeśli wasze operacje wymagają dostępności wieczorami i w weekendy, to ważny argument za B.
USER: Nasze magazyny pracują na 3 zmiany, więc SLA jest kluczowe. Idziemy z ofertą B, ale negocjuj cenę wdrożenia w dół.
output:
TEMAT: Porównanie dwóch ofert na system ERP
DOKUMENTY_ŹRÓDŁOWE: Oferta A (SoftHouse, SoftERP Pro) i Oferta B (CloudWorks, CloudERP Enterprise)
KLUCZOWE_FAKTY_Z_DOKUMENTÓW:
- Oferta A: licencja 120 000 PLN (50 stanowisk), wdrożenie 80 000 PLN, utrzymanie 18 000 PLN/rok, .NET 8 + MSSQL on-premise, SLA 99.9% reakcja 4h (dni robocze)
- Oferta B: SaaS 2 800 PLN/mies (60 stanowisk), wdrożenie 45 000 PLN, utrzymanie wliczone, cloud-native AWS, SLA 99.95% reakcja 1h (24/7), dodatkowy moduł e-Commerce
USTALENIA_Z_ROZMOWY:
- TCO 3 lata: A = 254 000 PLN, B = 145 800 PLN
- TCO 5 lat: A = 290 000 PLN, B = 213 000 PLN
- Po zakończeniu: A — system na własność, B — utrata dostępu
- SLA kluczowe — magazyny pracują 3 zmiany (24/7)
FEEDBACK_UŻYTKOWNIKA:
- Korekta: AI pominął aspekt własności systemu w modelu A
- "Nasze magazyny pracują na 3 zmiany" (kluczowy kontekst biznesowy)
ACTION_ITEMS:
- Negocjować cenę wdrożenia oferty B w dół
DECYZJE: Wybór oferty B (CloudERP Enterprise) ze względu na SLA 24/7

USER: Przeczytaj ten fragment regulaminu i powiedz czy jest zgodny z RODO.

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
REGULAMIN PROGRAMU LOJALNOŚCIOWEGO "PUNKTOMANIA"
(fragment — Rozdział IV: Dane osobowe)

§14. Dane osobowe
1. Administratorem danych osobowych Uczestników jest MegaShop S.A., ul. Handlowa 5, 01-001 Warszawa, NIP 1234567890.
2. Dane przetwarzane w ramach Programu: imię, nazwisko, adres e-mail, numer telefonu, adres zamieszkania, data urodzenia, PESEL, historia zakupów, preferencje produktowe.
3. Cel przetwarzania: realizacja Programu, marketing bezpośredni, profilowanie zakupowe, przekazywanie danych partnerom handlowym.
4. Dane przechowywane bezterminowo od momentu rejestracji w Programie.
5. Uczestnik wyraża zgodę na przetwarzanie danych poprzez przystąpienie do Programu.
6. Uczestnik może wycofać zgodę, co skutkuje natychmiastowym wykreśleniem z Programu i utratą zgromadzonych punktów.
7. Dane mogą być przekazywane do państw trzecich w ramach współpracy z partnerami zagranicznymi.
---END DOCUMENT---

ASSISTANT: Widzę kilka poważnych problemów z RODO: przechowywanie bezterminowe (§14.4), brak rozdzielenia zgód (§14.5), kara za wycofanie zgody (§14.6), brak informacji o transferze do państw trzecich (§14.7), i zbieranie PESEL bez uzasadnienia.
USER: PESEL jest nam potrzebny do weryfikacji tożsamości przy wydawaniu nagród powyżej 2000 PLN — to wymóg podatkowy.
ASSISTANT: OK, w takim razie PESEL jest uzasadniony, ale powinien być zbierany dopiero w momencie wydawania nagrody, nie przy rejestracji. W regulaminie trzeba to jasno określić jako podstawę prawną (obowiązek podatkowy).
USER: Racja. A co z tym bezterminowym przechowywaniem?
ASSISTANT: Bezterminowe przechowywanie narusza zasadę minimalizacji czasowej z art. 5 ust. 1 lit. e RODO. Musicie określić konkretny okres — np. 3 lata od ostatniej aktywności w programie.
USER: Daj mi listę wszystkich zmian które trzeba wprowadzić w tym fragmencie.
output:
TEMAT: Analiza zgodności regulaminu programu lojalnościowego z RODO
DOKUMENT_ŹRÓDŁOWY: Fragment regulaminu "Punktomania" (Rozdział IV, §14)
KLUCZOWE_FAKTY_Z_DOKUMENTU:
- Administrator: MegaShop S.A., NIP 1234567890
- Zbierane dane: imię, nazwisko, e-mail, telefon, adres, data urodzenia, PESEL, historia zakupów, preferencje
- Cele: realizacja programu, marketing bezpośredni, profilowanie, przekazywanie partnerom
- Przechowywanie: bezterminowe
- Zgoda: przez przystąpienie (jedna zgoda na wszystko)
- Wycofanie zgody = utrata punktów
- Transfer do państw trzecich bez szczegółów
USTALENIA_Z_ROZMOWY:
- §14.2: PESEL uzasadniony obowiązkiem podatkowym (nagrody >2000 PLN), ale zbierany za wcześnie
- §14.3: Brak rozdzielenia zgód (marketing, profilowanie, przekazywanie partnerom)
- §14.4: Bezterminowe przechowywanie narusza art. 5 ust. 1 lit. e RODO
- §14.5: Jedna zgoda na wszystko — narusza zasadę dobrowolności
- §14.6: Utrata punktów za wycofanie zgody = kara, niezgodna z RODO
- §14.7: Brak informacji o zabezpieczeniach transferu międzynarodowego
FEEDBACK_UŻYTKOWNIKA:
- "PESEL jest nam potrzebny do weryfikacji tożsamości" (kontekst — wymóg podatkowy, AI zaakceptował)
ACTION_ITEMS:
- Przygotować listę zmian do §14 regulaminu
- PESEL zbierać dopiero przy wydawaniu nagrody
- Określić okres retencji (propozycja: 3 lata od ostatniej aktywności)
- Rozdzielić zgody na osobne checkboxy
- Usunąć karę za wycofanie zgody
- Dodać szczegóły transferu międzynarodowego
DECYZJE: Regulamin wymaga kompleksowej przebudowy §14

Kancelaria Prawna Wiśniewski & Partners
ul. Nowy Świat 64/2, 00-357 Warszawa

Szanowni Państwo,

działając w imieniu i na rzecz mojego Klienta — BrandForce International Ltd. (UK Company No. 12345678), na podstawie udzielonego pełnomocnictwa, niniejszym informuję, że Państwa oznaczenie "TechFlow" używane w ramach oprogramowania do zarządzania projektami IT narusza prawa do znaku towarowego "TechFlow" zarejestrowanego przez mojego Klienta w EUIPO pod numerem EU018234567 w klasach 9, 35 i 42 Klasyfikacji Nicejskiej z datą pierwszeństwa 12.06.2022.

Z poważaniem,
adw. Piotr Wiśniewski

USER: Ale my używaliśmy nazwy "TechFlow" w Polsce od 2021 roku — mamy faktury i materiały marketingowe sprzed ich rejestracji. Tylko nie rejestrowaliśmy znaku.

USER: Dokładnie, mam faktury od marca 2021. Co robimy?
ASSISTANT: Proponuję: 1) Zebrać dowody używania od 2021 (faktury, materiały, archive.org, social media), 2) Zlecić własnej kancelarii odpowiedź powołującą się na art. 160 PWP, 3) Rozważyć zgłoszenie sprzeciwu wobec znaku EU lub rejestrację własnego znaku w UPRP.
USER: Brzmi rozsądnie. Zbieraj dowody i umów spotkanie z naszym prawnikiem na przyszły tydzień.

USER: Sprawdź ten raport z penetration testu naszej aplikacji.

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
RAPORT Z TESTÓW PENETRACYJNYCH
Zleceniodawca: FinApp Sp. z o.o.
Wykonawca: CyberShield Sp. z o.o.
Zakres: Aplikacja webowa app.finapp.pl + API (api.finapp.pl)
Okres testów: 10-21.03.2026
Metodyka: OWASP Testing Guide v4.2, PTES

WYNIKI — KRYTYCZNE:

VULN-001 [CRITICAL] SQL Injection w /api/v2/transactions?account_id=
- Endpoint /api/v2/transactions akceptuje parametr account_id bez sanityzacji
- PoC: GET /api/v2/transactions?account_id=1' OR '1'='1
- Wpływ: dostęp do transakcji WSZYSTKICH użytkowników
- CVSS: 9.8

VULN-002 [HIGH] Broken Authentication — brak rate limiting na /api/auth/login
- Możliwe brute-force loginu — 10 000 prób/min bez blokady
- CVSS: 7.5

VULN-003 [HIGH] IDOR w /api/v2/users/{id}/documents
- Zmiana ID w URL daje dostęp do dokumentów innych użytkowników
- PoC: Zalogowany jako user 1001, GET /api/v2/users/1002/documents — zwraca dokumenty usera 1002
- CVSS: 8.1

VULN-004 [MEDIUM] Sensitive data in JWT — token zawiera PESEL i nr konta bankowego
- JWT payload nie jest szyfrowany (JWS, nie JWE)
- CVSS: 5.3

VULN-005 [LOW] Missing security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options)
- CVSS: 3.1
---END DOCUMENT---

ASSISTANT: Sytuacja jest krytyczna. Masz SQL Injection z CVSS 9.8 i IDOR z CVSS 8.1 na produkcji — to oznacza pełny dostęp do danych wszystkich użytkowników. Priorytet absolutny to VULN-001 i VULN-003.
USER: VULN-001 już naprawiliśmy wczoraj — parametryzowane zapytania. Ale VULN-003 to nowy endpoint, który dopiero wdrożyliśmy tydzień temu.
ASSISTANT: Dobrze że VULN-001 jest już naprawiony. Dla VULN-003 musicie dodać weryfikację właściciela zasobu — porównanie ID z tokena JWT z ID w URL. To powinno być szybkie do wdrożenia.
USER: A co z tym JWT? PESEL w tokenie to duży problem — jesteśmy pod KNF.
ASSISTANT: Pod nadzorem KNF to faktycznie krytyczne — dane wrażliwe nie powinny być w JWS. Musicie albo przejść na JWE (szyfrowany JWT) albo usunąć wrażliwe dane z tokena i pobierać je server-side.
USER: Preferuję usunięcie z tokena — prościej. Daj mi plan naprawczy z priorytetami i terminami.
output:
TEMAT: Analiza raportu z testów penetracyjnych aplikacji FinApp
DOKUMENT_ŹRÓDŁOWY: Raport pentest CyberShield Sp. z o.o., okres 10-21.03.2026
KLUCZOWE_FAKTY_Z_DOKUMENTU:
- Zakres: app.finapp.pl + api.finapp.pl
- VULN-001 [CRITICAL, CVSS 9.8]: SQL Injection /api/v2/transactions?account_id=
- VULN-002 [HIGH, CVSS 7.5]: Brak rate limiting na /api/auth/login
- VULN-003 [HIGH, CVSS 8.1]: IDOR /api/v2/users/{id}/documents
- VULN-004 [MEDIUM, CVSS 5.3]: PESEL i nr konta w JWT (JWS, nie JWE)
- VULN-005 [LOW, CVSS 3.1]: Brak nagłówków bezpieczeństwa
USTALENIA_Z_ROZMOWY:
- VULN-001 już naprawiony (parametryzowane zapytania)
- VULN-003 dotyczy nowego endpointu (wdrożony tydzień temu)
- VULN-004 krytyczny ze względu na nadzór KNF — PESEL w JWT
- Decyzja: usunięcie wrażliwych danych z tokena (nie JWE)
FEEDBACK_UŻYTKOWNIKA:
- "VULN-001 już naprawiliśmy wczoraj" (aktualizacja statusu)
- "jesteśmy pod KNF" (kontekst regulacyjny podnoszący wagę VULN-004)
- "Preferuję usunięcie z tokena — prościej" (preferencja rozwiązania)
ACTION_ITEMS:
- VULN-003: Dodać weryfikację właściciela zasobu (porównanie ID z JWT vs URL)
- VULN-004: Usunąć PESEL i nr konta z JWT, pobieranie server-side
- VULN-002: Wdrożyć rate limiting na login
- VULN-005: Dodać nagłówki CSP, X-Frame-Options
- Przygotować plan naprawczy z priorytetami i terminami
DECYZJE: VULN-001 zamknięty, priorytet: VULN-003 → VULN-004 → VULN-002 → VULN-005

input:
USER: I got this lease amendment from our landlord. Can you review it?

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
FIRST AMENDMENT TO COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT

This Amendment ("Amendment") is entered into as of March 15, 2026, by and between:
Landlord: Meridian Properties LLC, 450 Park Avenue, Suite 2100, New York, NY 10022
Tenant: Quantum Analytics Inc., a Delaware corporation

Amending the Commercial Lease Agreement dated January 10, 2024 ("Original Lease") for the premises at 88 Chambers Street, 4th Floor, New York, NY 10007 (approx. 6,200 sq ft).

1. RENT ADJUSTMENT: Effective May 1, 2026, base monthly rent shall increase from $28,500 to $34,200 (20% increase), reflecting current market conditions.

2. LEASE TERM EXTENSION: The lease term is extended by 36 months, from the original expiration of December 31, 2027 to December 31, 2030.

3. RIGHT OF FIRST REFUSAL: Tenant shall have ROFR on the adjacent 5th floor space (~4,800 sq ft) at market rate, exercisable within 15 business days of written notice.

4. PERMITTED USE: Section 5.1 of the Original Lease is amended to include "data center operations" in addition to existing permitted uses.

5. IMPROVEMENT ALLOWANCE: Landlord shall provide a one-time tenant improvement allowance of $45,000 for buildout of the premises.

6. All other terms of the Original Lease remain unchanged.
---END DOCUMENT---

ASSISTANT: The amendment contains a significant 20% rent increase ($5,700/month more) in exchange for a 3-year extension, ROFR on the 5th floor, data center use permission, and a $45,000 improvement allowance. The net additional cost over 36 months is $205,200, partially offset by the $45,000 TI allowance.
USER: 20% is way too high. Market in Tribeca for Class B is more like 10-12% right now. Can we push back?
ASSISTANT: You're right — 10-12% is more aligned with current Tribeca Class B rates. I'd counter at 12% ($31,920/month instead of $34,200). That saves you $2,280/month or $82,080 over the 36-month extension.
USER: Good. Also, 15 business days for ROFR is too short — we need at least 30 to get board approval.
ASSISTANT: Agreed, 15 business days is tight for a board decision on ~4,800 sq ft of additional space. I'd push for 30 business days, with a clause allowing one 15-day extension if needed.
USER: Perfect. And the improvement allowance — $45k for 6,200 sq ft is only $7.26/sq ft. Industry standard for data center buildout is at least $15-20/sq ft. We need more.
ASSISTANT: Good catch. For data center requirements (raised floors, cooling, power infrastructure), $45k is insufficient. At $18/sq ft you'd need $111,600. I'd negotiate for at least $95,000.
USER: Let's counter with all three changes. Draft a response to the landlord.
output:
TOPIC: Review of commercial lease amendment — 88 Chambers Street, NYC
SOURCE_DOCUMENT: First Amendment to Commercial Lease, dated March 15, 2026
KEY_FACTS_FROM_DOCUMENT:
- Parties: Meridian Properties LLC (landlord) / Quantum Analytics Inc. (tenant)
- Premises: 88 Chambers St, 4th Floor, ~6,200 sq ft, New York
- Rent increase: $28,500 → $34,200/month (20%), effective May 1, 2026
- Term extension: 36 months (to Dec 31, 2030)
- ROFR: 5th floor (~4,800 sq ft), 15 business days to exercise
- New permitted use: data center operations added
- TI allowance: $45,000 one-time
DISCUSSION_FINDINGS:
- 20% rent increase above market (Tribeca Class B at 10-12%)
- Counter-proposal: 12% increase = $31,920/month (saves $82,080 over 36 months)
- ROFR 15 days too short for board approval — need 30 days + 15-day extension option
- TI allowance $7.26/sq ft too low for data center buildout (standard $15-20/sq ft)
- Counter TI: at least $95,000 (target $18/sq ft = $111,600)
USER_FEEDBACK:
- "20% is way too high. Market in Tribeca for Class B is more like 10-12%" (market knowledge correction)
- "15 business days for ROFR is too short — we need at least 30" (board governance constraint)
- "$45k for 6,200 sq ft is only $7.26/sq ft" (user calculated, corrected AI's oversight)
ACTION_ITEMS:
- Draft counter-proposal with three changes: rent 12%, ROFR 30 days, TI $95,000+
DECISIONS: Counter on all three points — rent at 12%, ROFR 30+15 days, TI minimum $95,000

USER: Can you review this SLA from our cloud provider? We're about to sign.

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT — NovusCloud Enterprise

1. AVAILABILITY
   - Platform SLA: 99.9% monthly uptime
   - Measurement: HTTP 200 responses from /health endpoint, measured every 60 seconds
   - Exclusions: scheduled maintenance (up to 8 hours/month), force majeure, customer-caused incidents
   - Planned maintenance window: Tuesday 02:00-06:00 UTC

2. PERFORMANCE
   - API response time: p95 < 200ms, p99 < 500ms
   - Data ingestion: up to 50,000 events/second sustained
   - Storage: unlimited, with 90-day hot retention, 365-day warm retention

3. SUPPORT
   - P1 (service down): response 15 min, resolution target 4 hours
   - P2 (degraded): response 1 hour, resolution target 8 hours
   - P3 (minor): response 4 hours, resolution target 5 business days

4. CREDITS
   - 99.0-99.9%: 10% credit on monthly fee
   - 95.0-99.0%: 25% credit
   - Below 95%: 50% credit
   - Maximum credit: 50% of monthly fee
   - Credits must be claimed within 30 days

5. DATA
   - Backup: daily snapshots, 30-day retention
   - RPO: 1 hour
   - RTO: 4 hours
   - Data residency: EU (Frankfurt), with failover to EU (Dublin)
---END DOCUMENT---

ASSISTANT: Overall this is a solid enterprise SLA. The 99.9% uptime, p95 < 200ms, and EU data residency look good. However, I see a few concerns: the 8 hours/month maintenance exclusion is generous for them, the credit cap at 50% is standard but could be higher, and the 30-day claim window is short.
USER: What about the RTO of 4 hours? We process real-time financial data — 4 hours of downtime could cost us millions.
ASSISTANT: For real-time financial data, 4 hours RTO is too long. You should negotiate for RTO of 1 hour or less, with automatic failover to Dublin. Also, the RPO of 1 hour means you could lose up to an hour of data — for financial data, you likely need RPO of 5-15 minutes.
USER: Exactly. RPO 1 hour is unacceptable for us. We need near-zero RPO. What about those maintenance windows?
ASSISTANT: The 8 hours/month maintenance exclusion effectively brings your real SLA down to about 98.9% in worst case. I'd push for: max 4 hours/month, 72-hour advance notice, and maintenance counted toward uptime SLA if it exceeds the window.
USER: Good points. Also, I just realized — there's nothing about data encryption at rest. That's a regulatory requirement for us.
ASSISTANT: Critical omission. For financial data you need: AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, customer-managed encryption keys (BYOK/CMK), and a clause about key rotation. Push for these as mandatory additions.
USER: Draft a redline list of all our requested changes.
output:
TOPIC: Review of NovusCloud Enterprise SLA before signing
SOURCE_DOCUMENT: NovusCloud Enterprise Service Level Agreement
KEY_FACTS_FROM_DOCUMENT:
- Uptime SLA: 99.9% monthly (HTTP 200 from /health, 60s intervals)
- Exclusions: up to 8h/month maintenance, force majeure, customer-caused
- Performance: API p95 < 200ms, p99 < 500ms, ingestion 50k events/sec
- Storage: unlimited, 90-day hot + 365-day warm retention
- Support: P1 response 15 min / resolution 4h, P2 1h/8h, P3 4h/5 days
- Credits: 10-50% depending on uptime, capped at 50%, 30-day claim window
- Data: daily backups (30-day retention), RPO 1h, RTO 4h, EU residency (Frankfurt + Dublin failover)
DISCUSSION_FINDINGS:
- RTO 4h unacceptable for real-time financial data — need ≤1h with auto failover
- RPO 1h unacceptable — need near-zero RPO (5-15 min max)
- 8h/month maintenance exclusion too generous — negotiate to 4h, 72h notice, count overages in SLA
- No encryption at rest clause — critical omission for financial regulatory compliance
- 30-day credit claim window is short
USER_FEEDBACK:
- "We process real-time financial data — 4 hours of downtime could cost us millions" (business context)
- "RPO 1 hour is unacceptable" (firm requirement)
- "there's nothing about data encryption at rest" (user caught critical omission)
ACTION_ITEMS:
- Draft redline list of requested SLA changes
- RTO: ≤1 hour with automatic failover
- RPO: near-zero (5-15 minutes)
- Maintenance: max 4h/month, 72h advance notice
- Add encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3, BYOK/CMK, key rotation
- Extend credit claim window
DECISIONS: Do not sign until all redline items are addressed

input:
USER: Look at this vendor security questionnaire response. Something feels off.

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
VENDOR SECURITY QUESTIONNAIRE — DataSync Corp.
Completed: March 20, 2026

Q1: Do you encrypt data at rest?
A: Yes, all data is encrypted using industry-standard encryption.

Q2: What encryption algorithm and key length do you use?
A: AES-256 for data at rest, TLS 1.2+ for data in transit.

Q3: Where is customer data stored?
A: Customer data is stored in SOC 2 Type II certified data centers.

Q4: Do you support SSO/SAML?
A: Yes, we support SAML 2.0 and OIDC integration.

Q5: What is your data retention policy?
A: Data is retained for the duration of the contract plus 90 days.

Q6: Do you perform regular penetration testing?
A: Yes, we perform annual penetration testing by a third-party firm.

Q7: Do you have a SOC 2 Type II report?
A: Yes, our most recent report is available upon request under NDA.

Q8: How do you handle data subject access requests (DSAR)?
A: We provide tools for customers to manage DSARs within 30 days.

Q9: Do you subcontract any data processing?
A: We may use subprocessors for specific functions. A current list is available on our website.

Q10: What is your incident response time?
A: We notify affected customers within 72 hours of a confirmed breach.
---END DOCUMENT---

ASSISTANT: Several answers are vague and evasive. Q1 says "industry-standard" without specifics. Q3 doesn't name the actual location/region. Q6 — annual pentesting is infrequent for a data processor. Q9 is a non-answer about subprocessors.
USER: Exactly what I was thinking. Q3 especially — they didn't say WHERE the data is. We need it in EU only.
ASSISTANT: Right. "SOC 2 certified data centers" tells you about their audit standard, not data residency. You need explicit confirmation: EU-only storage, no replication to US/other regions, and contractual commitment to EU data residency.
USER: Also Q10 — 72 hours is the GDPR maximum. That's them doing the bare minimum. Our policy requires notification within 24 hours.
ASSISTANT: Good catch. 72 hours is the legal minimum under GDPR, not a proactive stance. You should require: initial notification within 24 hours, detailed incident report within 48 hours, and root cause analysis within 14 days.
USER: And Q9 — "may use subprocessors" is too vague. Under GDPR we need to approve each subprocessor.
ASSISTANT: Exactly. You need: a complete, current list of subprocessors, advance notification (30+ days) before adding new ones, the right to object, and contractual flow-down of security requirements.
USER: OK send them back a detailed follow-up questionnaire focusing on these gaps.
output:
TOPIC: Analysis of vendor security questionnaire — DataSync Corp.
SOURCE_DOCUMENT: Vendor Security Questionnaire completed March 20, 2026
KEY_FACTS_FROM_DOCUMENT:
- Vendor: DataSync Corp.
- Encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.2+ in transit
- Data location: "SOC 2 Type II certified data centers" (no specific region)
- Auth: SAML 2.0 + OIDC
- Retention: contract duration + 90 days
- Pentesting: annual, third-party
- SOC 2 Type II: available under NDA
- DSAR: tools provided, 30-day window
- Subprocessors: "may use" — list on website
- Breach notification: 72 hours
DISCUSSION_FINDINGS:
- Q1/Q3: Vague answers — "industry-standard" and no data residency specified
- Q3: No EU data residency commitment — critical gap
- Q6: Annual pentesting insufficient for data processor
- Q9: Subprocessor answer non-compliant — GDPR requires specific approval
- Q10: 72h is GDPR minimum — company policy requires 24h notification
USER_FEEDBACK:
- "they didn't say WHERE the data is. We need it in EU only" (firm requirement)
- "72 hours is the GDPR maximum. That's them doing the bare minimum" (user recognized evasion)
- "may use subprocessors is too vague. Under GDPR we need to approve each subprocessor" (GDPR knowledge)
ACTION_ITEMS:
- Send follow-up questionnaire demanding specifics on: EU data residency, subprocessor list, advance notification for new subprocessors, 24h breach notification, more frequent pentesting
- Require contractual EU data residency commitment
- Require right to object to new subprocessors
- Request latest SOC 2 Type II report under NDA
DECISIONS: Vendor not approved until gaps are addressed with contractual commitments

USER: We received two competing proposals for our new office network. Help me compare.

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
PROPOSAL A — NetPrime Solutions
Project: Office Network Infrastructure — 150 users, 3 floors

Equipment:
- Core switch: Cisco Catalyst 9300 stack (2x) — $18,400
- Access switches: Cisco Catalyst 9200 (12x) — $36,000
- Wireless: Cisco Meraki MR46 (24x) — $28,800
- Firewall: Palo Alto PA-850 (HA pair) — $32,000
- UPS: APC Smart-UPS 3000VA (4x) — $8,800

Services:
- Design & planning: $12,000
- Installation & cabling: $28,000
- Configuration & testing: $18,000
- Training (2 days): $4,000

Total: $186,000
Warranty: 3 years (Cisco SmartNet + Meraki license)
SLA: 4-hour on-site response, 99.99% uptime guarantee
Timeline: 6 weeks from PO
---END DOCUMENT---

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
PROPOSAL B — CloudNet Systems
Project: Office Network Infrastructure — 150 users, 3 floors

Equipment:
- Core switch: Juniper EX4400 stack (2x) — $14,200
- Access switches: Juniper EX2300 (10x) — $22,000
- Wireless: Juniper Mist AP43 (20x) — $18,000
- Firewall: Fortinet FortiGate 200F (HA pair) — $22,000
- UPS: Eaton 5PX 3000VA (4x) — $7,200

Services:
- Design & planning: $8,000
- Installation & cabling: $22,000
- Configuration & testing: $14,000
- Training (1 day): $2,000

Total: $129,400
Warranty: 1 year manufacturer, extended to 3 years for +$12,000
SLA: Next business day on-site, 99.95% uptime guarantee
Timeline: 4 weeks from PO
---END DOCUMENT---

ASSISTANT: Proposal A (NetPrime/Cisco+Palo Alto) is $56,600 more expensive but includes premium SLA (4-hour vs next-day response), higher uptime guarantee (99.99% vs 99.95%), more APs (24 vs 20), and 3-year warranty included. Proposal B (CloudNet/Juniper+Fortinet) is more budget-friendly with a faster timeline.
USER: We have a strict $150k budget. So Proposal A is out unless they can cut significantly.
ASSISTANT: With $150k budget, Proposal B fits at $129,400 (or $141,400 with extended warranty). For Proposal A to fit, they'd need to cut $36,000+ which seems unlikely without downgrading equipment.
USER: Actually the Juniper Mist AI is really appealing — we've had nothing but trouble with managing our current WiFi. How does Mist compare to Meraki?
ASSISTANT: Juniper Mist and Meraki are both cloud-managed, but Mist's AI engine (Marvis) is generally considered more advanced for proactive troubleshooting. It uses machine learning to identify issues before users report them. Meraki is simpler to manage but less intelligent in diagnostics.
USER: That settles it. Go with Proposal B but add the 3-year warranty. Also ask if they can add 4 more APs — 20 might not be enough for 150 users across 3 floors.
output:
TOPIC: Comparison of two office network infrastructure proposals
SOURCE_DOCUMENTS: Proposal A (NetPrime Solutions) and Proposal B (CloudNet Systems)
KEY_FACTS_FROM_DOCUMENTS:
- Proposal A (Cisco/Palo Alto): $186,000 — 2x C9300 core, 12x C9200 access, 24x Meraki MR46 APs, PA-850 HA firewall, 3-year warranty included, 4h on-site SLA, 99.99% uptime, 6-week timeline
- Proposal B (Juniper/Fortinet): $129,400 — 2x EX4400 core, 10x EX2300 access, 20x Mist AP43 APs, FortiGate 200F HA firewall, 1-year warranty (+$12k for 3yr), next-day SLA, 99.95% uptime, 4-week timeline
- Price difference: $56,600 (A is 43% more expensive)
DISCUSSION_FINDINGS:
- Budget cap: $150k — Proposal A exceeds by $36k, Proposal B fits at $141,400 with extended warranty
- Juniper Mist AI (Marvis) preferred over Meraki for proactive WiFi troubleshooting
- 20 APs may be insufficient for 150 users on 3 floors — request 4 additional
USER_FEEDBACK:
- "We have a strict $150k budget" (hard constraint, eliminates Proposal A)
- "we've had nothing but trouble with managing our current WiFi" (pain point — Mist AI addresses this)
ACTION_ITEMS:
- Select Proposal B with 3-year extended warranty ($141,400)
- Request quote for 4 additional Mist AP43 APs
- Verify 24 APs sufficient for 150 users / 3 floors coverage
DECISIONS: Proposal B (CloudNet/Juniper+Fortinet) selected, with 3-year warranty and additional APs requested

input:
USER: I got this incident postmortem from the SRE team. Let's discuss the follow-ups.

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
INCIDENT POSTMORTEM — INC-2026-0312
Severity: SEV-1
Date: March 12, 2026 14:23-17:45 UTC (3h 22min)
Impact: Complete API outage for all customers (est. 12,000 affected users)
Financial impact: ~$180,000 estimated revenue loss

Timeline:
14:23 — Automated deploy of auth-service v2.14.0 via GitHub Actions
14:24 — Health check passes (checks /health, not /ready)
14:27 — PagerDuty alert: 5xx error rate > 50% on auth-service
14:32 — On-call SRE (Jamie) acknowledges alert
14:45 — Jamie identifies auth-service v2.14.0 as cause, attempts rollback
14:52 — Rollback fails: database migration in v2.14.0 is not backward-compatible
15:10 — Incident escalated to SEV-1, war room opened
15:45 — Root cause identified: migration added NOT NULL constraint to users.org_id without backfilling existing rows
16:30 — Hotfix branch created: backfill NULL org_id values with default
17:15 — Hotfix deployed and verified
17:45 — Full service recovery confirmed

Root cause: DB migration in auth-service v2.14.0 added NOT NULL constraint on users.org_id. ~2,300 legacy user rows had NULL org_id. Every auth query involving these users returned 500.

Contributing factors:
1. Migration not tested against production-like data (staging DB has no NULL org_ids)
2. Health check endpoint (/health) only verifies service is running, not functional readiness (/ready)
3. No automated rollback on 5xx spike
4. Rollback impossible due to irreversible migration
---END DOCUMENT---

ASSISTANT: This is a textbook deployment-caused outage. Key issues: untested migration, health check that doesn't actually check health, no automated rollback, and irreversible migrations. The 3h22min outage for a SEV-1 is long — most of that time was spent discovering the rollback was impossible.
USER: Exactly. The rollback failure is what turned a 20-minute incident into a 3-hour one. We need to make migrations reversible.
ASSISTANT: Agreed. I'd recommend: 1) All migrations must have a working down/rollback step tested in CI, 2) Separate schema changes from data changes (deploy constraint AFTER backfill), 3) Use expand-contract pattern for breaking changes.
USER: Good. But the staging data issue is just as important — staging should mirror prod data patterns. How do we fix that?
ASSISTANT: Options: 1) Sanitized prod data copy to staging (weekly sync with PII scrubbed), 2) Synthetic data generator that matches prod distributions, 3) Migration dry-run against prod snapshot in CI. Option 1 is most reliable but has compliance implications.
USER: We're SOC 2 so sanitized copy is fine as long as we scrub PII properly. Let's go with option 1 plus the dry-run in CI as a safety net.
ASSISTANT: Smart layered approach. For the health check issue, you should also implement /ready endpoints that actually test database connectivity and query execution, not just process liveness.
USER: Yes. Prioritize these action items and assign rough timelines.
output:
TOPIC: Discussion of SEV-1 incident postmortem — auth-service outage INC-2026-0312
SOURCE_DOCUMENT: Incident Postmortem INC-2026-0312, March 12, 2026
KEY_FACTS_FROM_DOCUMENT:
- Severity: SEV-1, duration 3h 22min (14:23-17:45 UTC)
- Impact: complete API outage, ~12,000 users, ~$180,000 revenue loss
- Cause: auth-service v2.14.0 DB migration added NOT NULL on users.org_id — 2,300 legacy rows had NULL
- Rollback failed: migration was irreversible
- Health check only verified liveness (/health), not readiness (/ready)
- Staging DB had no NULL org_ids — migration not tested against prod-like data
- No automated rollback on 5xx spike
- Hotfix: backfill NULL org_id with default value
DISCUSSION_FINDINGS:
- Rollback failure extended incident from ~20 min to 3h22min
- All migrations must have tested rollback steps
- Use expand-contract pattern for breaking schema changes
- Staging fix: sanitized prod data copy (weekly, PII scrubbed) — SOC 2 compatible
- Additional safety: migration dry-run against prod snapshot in CI
- /ready endpoint must test actual DB connectivity
USER_FEEDBACK:
- "The rollback failure is what turned a 20-minute incident into a 3-hour one" (correctly prioritized root cause)
- "staging should mirror prod data patterns" (identified equally important issue)
- "We're SOC 2 so sanitized copy is fine" (compliance context)
ACTION_ITEMS:
- Require reversible migrations with tested down steps in CI
- Implement expand-contract pattern for breaking changes
- Set up weekly sanitized prod→staging data sync (PII scrubbed)
- Add migration dry-run against prod snapshot in CI pipeline
- Implement /ready endpoints with actual DB query checks
- Add automated rollback trigger on 5xx rate threshold
- Assign priorities and timelines to all items
DECISIONS: Layered approach — sanitized prod data + CI dry-run; all migrations must be reversible

---BEGIN DOCUMENT---
DEVIS N° DEV-2026-0445
Agence WebCréa
15 rue du Commerce, 75015 Paris
SIRET: 823 456 789 00012

Client: Maison Laurent — Pâtisserie artisanale
22 avenue Victor Hugo, 69002 Lyon

   - Audit du site existant: 1 200 EUR
   - Maquettes desktop + mobile (8 pages): 3 800 EUR
   - 2 allers-retours de corrections: inclus

2. Développement
   - Intégration HTML/CSS responsive: 4 500 EUR
   - CMS WordPress + thème sur mesure: 3 200 EUR
   - Module boutique en ligne (WooCommerce): 2 800 EUR
   - Module de réservation en ligne (Click & Collect): 1 500 EUR
   - Formulaire de contact + newsletter: 600 EUR

3. Contenu
   - Shooting photo professionnel (1 journée): 1 800 EUR

   - Certificat SSL: inclus
   - Migration des données existantes: 800 EUR
   - Formation administration (2h): 400 EUR

Total HT: 22 380 EUR

Total TTC: 26 856 EUR

Délai: 10 semaines
Acompte: 40% à la commande (8 952 EUR HT)
Conditions: 30% après validation des maquettes, 30% à la livraison
---END DOCUMENT---

ASSISTANT: Le devis est détaillé et bien structuré. Pour une refonte complète avec e-commerce et Click & Collect, 22 380 EUR HT est dans la fourchette du marché parisien. Les postes principaux sont le développement (12 600 EUR) et le design (5 000 EUR).
USER: WordPress + WooCommerce pour une pâtisserie, c'est pas un peu overkill ? On a 30 produits maximum.
ASSISTANT: Pour 30 produits, WooCommerce fonctionne bien mais c'est effectivement lourd. Les alternatives seraient Shopify (plus simple, mais abonnement mensuel) ou un site statique avec Snipcart (moins cher, plus rapide). Cependant, WordPress vous donne plus de flexibilité et d'indépendance.
USER: On reste sur WordPress, c'est ce que notre équipe sait gérer. Mais 3 200 EUR pour le thème sur mesure — on ne pourrait pas utiliser un thème premium à 60 EUR et l'adapter ?
ASSISTANT: Un thème premium adapté coûterait environ 800-1 200 EUR d'adaptation au lieu de 3 200 EUR sur mesure. L'inconvénient : dépendance aux mises à jour du thème et design moins unique. Pour une pâtisserie artisanale qui mise sur l'image, le sur mesure se justifie.
USER: Notre image de marque est cruciale — on garde le sur mesure. Mais le shooting photo 1 800 EUR me semble cher. Mon neveu est photographe, il peut le faire pour 500 EUR.
ASSISTANT: Attention — la qualité des photos de pâtisserie est essentielle pour le e-commerce. Un photographe spécialisé en food photography maîtrise l'éclairage, le styling et le post-traitement spécifiques. Si votre neveu a de l'expérience en photo culinaire, c'est une option. Sinon, je déconseille l'économie sur ce poste.
USER: Il fait surtout du portrait... Tu as raison, gardons le shooting pro. Par contre, négocie la formation de 2h à 4h pour le même prix — 2h c'est insuffisant pour WordPress + WooCommerce.

La rescisión anticipada por parte del Cliente conllevará el pago del 60% de las mensualidades restantes.

CLÁUSULA 6 — KPIs
El Prestador se compromete a reportar mensualmente, sin objetivos mínimos vinculantes.
---END DOCUMENT---

ASSISTANT: Veo varios problemas serios. La cláusula 4 sobre propiedad intelectual es muy desfavorable — ellos se quedan con todo el contenido que crean para vosotros. La penalización del 60% es abusiva. Y la cláusula 6 no tiene KPIs vinculantes, lo cual es inusual para un contrato de marketing.
USER: ¡Exacto! La cláusula 4 es inaceptable. Nosotros pagamos, nosotros somos los dueños del contenido.
ASSISTANT: Correcto. La cláusula 4 debe modificarse para que la propiedad intelectual sea del Cliente desde el momento de la creación y pago. El Prestador puede retener derecho a usar los trabajos en su portfolio, con autorización previa.
USER: Y ese bonus del 15% sobre incremento de facturación — ¿cómo se mide eso? Es muy subjetivo.
ASSISTANT: Exacto, la atribución de facturación a "campañas digitales" es ambigua y fuente de conflictos. Debéis definir: modelo de atribución específico (last click, multi-touch), herramienta de medición (Google Analytics 4), período de atribución, y un cap máximo al bonus mensual.
USER: Buena observación. Pon un cap de 2.000 EUR/mes al bonus. Y cambia la penalización del 60% al 25%.
ASSISTANT: Resumo los cambios: PI al Cliente, bonus con atribución definida y cap 2.000 EUR/mes, penalización reducida al 25%, y KPIs vinculantes con objetivos mínimos.
USER: Perfecto. Redacta las contraprouestas para enviar a su abogado.

- PI: propiedad del Prestador, licencia no exclusiva al Cliente
- Penalización: 60% de mensualidades restantes
- KPIs: reporte mensual sin objetivos vinculantes
CONCLUSIONES_DE_LA_DISCUSIÓN:

- Penalización 60% abusiva

ASSISTANT: Предложение структурировано, все позиции расписаны. Общая стоимость 3 575 000 руб. без НДС за 420 м² — это примерно 8 500 руб./м². Для Москвы и БЦ класса А это в рамках рынка, но есть что обсудить.
USER: 50% предоплата — это много. Обычно мы платим не больше 30%.
ASSISTANT: Согласен, 50% предоплата — это высокий риск. Стандартная схема: 30% аванс, 40% по промежуточной приёмке, 30% по окончании работ. Это мотивирует подрядчика и снижает ваши риски.
USER: Именно. А что с ковролином — 350 000 за 420 м² это около 833 руб./м². Нормально?
ASSISTANT: 833 руб./м² за ковролин с укладкой — это средняя цена. Зависит от класса: коммерческий класс 33 обычно стоит 600-900 руб./м² с работой. Цена адекватная.
USER: Хорошо. Но сплит-системы — 4 штуки на 420 м²? Мне кажется мало для открытого офиса.
ASSISTANT: Для 420 м² нужно минимум 6-8 сплит-систем (или 2 мультизональные VRF-системы). 4 сплита — это ~105 м² на каждый, что недостаточно для комфортной температуры, особенно летом. Нужно пересчитать тепловую нагрузку.
USER: Точно. Попроси их пересчитать кондиционирование и снизить предоплату до 30%.

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 20, 2026

Between:
Quantum Dynamics Ltd., registered at 45 Canary Wharf, London E14 5AB, United Kingdom, Company No. 12345678
("Disclosing Party")

and:

Atlas Robotics Inc., 880 Third Avenue, New York, NY 10022, USA, EIN 87-1234567
("Receiving Party")

1. PURPOSE
The parties wish to explore a potential joint venture in autonomous warehouse robotics ("Purpose"). During these discussions, confidential information may be shared.

2. DEFINITION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
All technical data, trade secrets, business plans, financial information, customer lists, product designs, source code, algorithms, and any information marked "Confidential" or reasonably understood to be confidential.

3. OBLIGATIONS
The Receiving Party shall:
a) Use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose
b) Not disclose to third parties without prior written consent
c) Limit access to employees with a need-to-know basis
d) Apply same degree of care as for its own confidential information (minimum: reasonable care)

4. EXCLUSIONS
Information that: (a) is publicly available, (b) was known prior to disclosure, (c) is independently developed, (d) is required by law or court order (with prompt notice to Disclosing Party).

5. TERM
This Agreement remains in effect for 3 years from the date of execution. Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after termination.

6. REMEDIES
Breach may cause irreparable harm. Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to other remedies. Liquidated damages: £500,000 per breach.

7. GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales. Disputes shall be resolved by arbitration under LCIA rules in London.

Quantum Dynamics Ltd. — Dr. Sarah Mitchell, CEO
Atlas Robotics Inc. — James Rodriguez, General Counsel

Datenschutzbeauftragter:
Rechtsanwalt Thomas Fischer
Maximilianstraße 12, 80539 München
E-Mail: dsb@fischer-recht.de

Stand: 15. März 2026

1. Erhebung und Verarbeitung personenbezogener Daten
Wir verarbeiten folgende Kategorien personenbezogener Daten:
- Bestandsdaten (Name, Adresse, Geburtsdatum)
- Kontaktdaten (E-Mail, Telefon)
- Gesundheitsdaten (nur mit ausdrücklicher Einwilligung, Art. 9 Abs. 2 lit. a DSGVO)
- Nutzungsdaten (IP-Adresse, Browsertyp, Zugriffszeiten)
- Zahlungsdaten (Bankverbindung, Rechnungsadresse)

2. Rechtsgrundlagen
- Art. 6 Abs. 1 lit. a DSGVO (Einwilligung)
- Art. 6 Abs. 1 lit. b DSGVO (Vertragserfüllung)
- Art. 6 Abs. 1 lit. f DSGVO (Berechtigtes Interesse)
- Art. 9 Abs. 2 lit. a DSGVO (Einwilligung für Gesundheitsdaten)

3. Empfänger der Daten
- Hosting-Provider: Hetzner Online GmbH (Standort: Deutschland)
- Zahlungsdienstleister: Stripe Payments Europe Ltd.
- E-Mail-Service: Mailgun Technologies Inc. (Standardvertragsklauseln)
- Keine Weitergabe an sonstige Dritte ohne Einwilligung

4. Speicherdauer
- Vertragsdaten: 10 Jahre nach Vertragsende (handels- und steuerrechtliche Pflichten)
- Gesundheitsdaten: Löschung 30 Tage nach Zweckerfüllung
- Nutzungsdaten: 90 Tage
- Einwilligungen: bis zum Widerruf

5. Betroffenenrechte
Sie haben das Recht auf: Auskunft (Art. 15), Berichtigung (Art. 16), Löschung (Art. 17), Einschränkung (Art. 18), Datenübertragbarkeit (Art. 20), Widerspruch (Art. 21), Beschwerde bei der Aufsichtsbehörde (Bayerisches Landesamt für Datenschutzaufsicht).

6. Cookies
Technisch notwendige Cookies: ohne Einwilligung. Analyse- und Marketing-Cookies: nur mit Einwilligung (Cookie-Banner). Opt-out jederzeit möglich.

7. Drittlandtransfer
Datenübermittlung an Mailgun (USA) auf Basis von EU-Standardvertragsklauseln (SCC) gemäß Art. 46 Abs. 2 lit. c DSGVO.
